On 16/12/2025 midnight while traveling to Tuljapur, we discovered a check light of TPMS sensor. Even after checking tyre pressure the check light didnt switch off. On 18/12/2025 i rushed to Sudarshan Tata Service Centre, Bhiwandi for the same. I got my car serviced same day.
On 20/12/2025 my brand-new Tata Curvv, purchased on 30th November 2025. It is deeply alarming and unacceptable that a vehicle which is less than 15 days old has already suffered a complete breakdown.
On 20th December 2025, while travelling with my family—including children—in Lonavala, the vehicle suddenly failed to start and displayed a “DCA Fault” warning. As a result, my entire family was stranded on the roadside for nearly 5–6 hours, waiting for assistance from Tata Motors Roadside Assistance.
This incident was extremely distressing, unsafe, and humiliating, especially with children present. What worsened the situation was the highly delayed and unsatisfactory response from the RSA team, which is unacceptable for a brand-new vehicle under warranty.
The car has now been shifted to the nearest authorized Tata Motors service center, however, I wish to clearly state that I am not willing to accept repeated repairs, temporary fixes, or assurances for a vehicle that has failed within days of purchase.
A complete breakdown due to a DCA fault in a 15-day-old car strongly indicates a manufacturing or critical system defect. Such an incident has completely eroded my trust in the safety and reliability of this vehicle.

Hello Team Tata Motors,
I am a Tata Nexon owner, purchased brand new in August 2022 for approximately ₹12 lakh. My vehicle has been driven only 19,000 km in over three years and has been well maintained.
Recently, when I approached an authorized Tata showroom for an exchange offer to upgrade to a Tata Safari, I was quoted a value of only ₹5.5 lakh. A depreciation of nearly 50% despite such low usage is extremely disappointing and does not reflect the condition or value of my vehicle.
It is concerning that even Tata dealerships do not seem to fairly value Tata’s own products, which raises serious questions about resale assurance and customer retention. As a customer looking to upgrade within the Tata brand, I expected a more reasonable and transparent valuation or a loyalty-based exchange benefit.
I request Tata Motors to kindly review this concern and explain how exchange values are assessed, and whether there are any customer-friendly upgrade or loyalty programs for existing Tata owners.
Looking forward to your response.
Regards,
Abdul Naheem
Tata Nexon Owner

Initially it was regarding the unacceptable delay in the delivery of my Tata Curvv car (Model- Pure + S-DCA, Colour- Daytona Grey, Petrol), booked on 3rd October 2025.
At the time of booking, I was clearly informed that the delivery would be done on 16th October 2025. I have already paid the booking amount of ₹50,000, and my loan has been sanctioned well in advance. However, just before the delivery date, the dealership postponed it first to 22nd October, and then again to 2nd November — to which I agreed patiently.
Now, on 29th October, I have been informed yet again that even the 2nd November delivery will not be possible. This repeated shifting of dates without any accountability was completely unprofessional and unacceptable from a brand like Tata Motors.
I have been waiting for nearly a month now, traveling daily from Ambernath to Thane by local train because of this delay. Being a doctor, my work schedule is already demanding, and this ongoing delay has caused immense inconvenience. Despite multiple follow-ups, there has been no accountability or proactive communication from Tata Motors or the dealership.
I got my vehicle delivered on 30/11/2025. We reached showroom at 12:30 pm and we got our car at 6:00 pm. It was a precious day for us so we decided to stay calm and accept the delivery.

I am a Tata Nexon owner, purchased brand new in August 2022 for approximately ₹12 lakh. My vehicle has been driven only 19,000 km in over three years and has been well maintained.

I am writing this with deep disappointment as a loyal Tata customer. Until today, I have always felt proud to own and drive a Tata car. However, my recent experience has left me extremely concerned.

I own a Tata Altroz (2022 model), and for the second time within a single year, I am facing a fuel tank leakage issue. The fuel tank was already replaced once, and despite the replacement being done within 10 months, the same defect has reoccurred. This clearly raises serious concerns about the quality and durability of the fuel tank used in this model.

What is more disheartening is that the service centre is now denying free replacement, even though this is a repeated manufacturing-related issue. This means in a brand-new Tata Curvv:
 Same issue multiple times
 Trips cancelled twice

 No compensation
 Long waiting due to “lack of spare parts”

I purchased a Tata Harrier XZA+ in 2023. Since March 2024 the vehicle has suffered recurring, serious faults that Tata authorised service centres have repeatedly failed to fix. I have endured repeated breakdowns, multiple part changes, significant out‑of‑pocket expense, loss of time, disruption to my official duties in the armed forces, and ongoing mental distress. Tata Motors must now either replace the vehicle with an equivalent new vehicle or refund the full purchase price plus all consequential costs and losses. This is my final opportunity to have Tata resolve this matter amicably.
Chronology of defects and service attempts
- March–May 2024: Drive modes repeatedly disabled while travelling (first failure on route Gwalior–Jaipur). Vehicle taken to Roshan Tata Motors (22 Godam) and later Velocity Tata Motors Gwalior. Service centres performed repeated DPF/engine regeneration procedures but the fault recurred within 100–200 km. I visited service centres approximately 6–7 times; problem persisted. 
- During these visits service personnel ultimately advised that a sensor wiring was damaged. I doubt whether the wiring was originally damaged or tampered with while in their custody. A sensor had to be procured through insurance; delivery was delayed and incorrect parts were initially supplied. To allow urgent travel I was given a used sensor taken from another vehicle. The vehicle ran ~1,200 km and then the same drive‑mode fault recurred. 
- Pune 2024–2025: Multiple visits to Panchjanya Automobile Warje for the same recurring fault; identical temporary remedies were applied and the fault returned. 
- Oct 2024: Vehicle battery drained while idle; RSA jumpstart obtained. Service centre replaced battery in December 2024 on their recommendation without clear root‑cause diagnosis. Battery failures continued despite normal usage. 
- July–Aug 2025: RSA jumpstarts required in Delhi; Sagar Tata Motors charged Rs. 23,000 for servicing and diagnostics addressing battery drain. Fault recurred within a month; SC later found current leakage and purportedly rectified it. 
- 18 Oct 2025: Vehicle would not start; RSA jumpstart called again. 
- 23 Oct 2025: Vehicle retained
Not judged. Chosen.

मैं यह पत्र भारत के अनमोल रत्न, स्वर्गीय श्री रतन टाटा जी को भावभीनी श्रद्धांजलि अर्पित करते हुए लिख रहा हूँ। रतन टाटा जी ने न केवल भारतीय उद्योग को ऊंचाइयों पर पहुँचाया, बल्कि उन्होंने हम जैसे करोड़ों भारतीयों को बड़े सपने देखना सिखाया।
टाटा सिएरा मेरे लिए महज एक गाड़ी नहीं, बल्कि रतन टाटा जी की दूरगामी सोच और 'स्वदेशी' गौरव का प्रतीक है। जब 90 के दशक में पहली बार सिएरा आई थी, तो वह समय से बहुत आगे थी। अब जब सिएरा अपने नए इलेक्ट्रिक अवतार में वापस आ रही है, तो यह हम प्रशंसकों के लिए एक भावुक क्षण है।
मैं टाटा ब्रांड का एक समर्पित प्रशंसक हूँ और रतन टाटा जी के जीवन मूल्यों से अत्यधिक प्रेरित हूँ। मेरा एक सपना है कि मैं इस नई पीढ़ी की सिएरा को चलाऊँ। मैं जानता हूँ कि यह एक असामान्य अनुरोध है, लेकिन क्या टाटा मोटर्स अपने सबसे छोटे प्रशंसक के लिए कोई ऐसी योजना या विशेष विचार रखती है जहाँ मुझे इस ऐतिहासिक वाहन से जुड़ने का अवसर मिल सके? मैं इसे रतन टाटा जी की विरासत के सम्मान के रूप में अपने पास रखना चाहता हूँ।
रतन टाटा जी हमेशा कहते थे, "लोहे को कोई नष्ट नहीं कर सकता, लेकिन उसकी अपनी जंग उसे नष्ट कर देती है।" उनकी इसी सोच ने मुझे हमेशा आगे बढ़ने की प्रेरणा दी है।
मैं आपकी प्रतिक्रिया और टाटा सिएरा के आगमन की उत्सुकता से प्रतीक्षा कर रहा हूँ।

मैं यह पत्र भारत के अनमोल रत्न, स्वर्गीय श्री रतन टाटा जी को भावभीनी श्रद्धांजलि अर्पित करते हुए लिख रहा हूँ। रतन टाटा जी ने न केवल भारतीय उद्योग को ऊंचाइयों पर पहुँचाया, बल्कि उन्होंने हम जैसे करोड़ों भारतीयों को बड़े सपने देखना सिखाया।
टाटा सिएरा मेरे लिए महज एक गाड़ी नहीं, बल्कि रतन टाटा जी की दूरगामी सोच और 'स्वदेशी' गौरव का प्रतीक है। जब 90 के दशक में पहली बार सिएरा आई थी, तो वह समय से बहुत आगे थी। अब जब सिएरा अपने नए इलेक्ट्रिक अवतार में वापस आ रही है, तो यह हम प्रशंसकों के लिए एक भावुक क्षण है।
मैं टाटा ब्रांड का एक समर्पित प्रशंसक हूँ और रतन टाटा जी के जीवन मूल्यों से अत्यधिक प्रेरित हूँ। मेरा एक सपना है कि मैं इस नई पीढ़ी की सिएरा को चलाऊँ। मैं जानता हूँ कि यह एक असामान्य अनुरोध है, लेकिन क्या टाटा मोटर्स अपने सबसे छोटे प्रशंसक के लिए कोई ऐसी योजना या विशेष विचार रखती है जहाँ मुझे इस ऐतिहासिक वाहन से जुड़ने का अवसर मिल सके? मैं इसे रतन टाटा जी की विरासत के सम्मान के रूप में अपने पास रखना चाहता हूँ।
रतन टाटा जी हमेशा कहते थे, "लोहे को कोई नष्ट नहीं कर सकता, लेकिन उसकी अपनी जंग उसे नष्ट कर देती है।" उनकी इसी सोच ने मुझे हमेशा आगे बढ़ने की प्रेरणा दी है।
मैं आपकी प्रतिक्रिया और टाटा सिएरा के आगमन की उत्सुकता से प्रतीक्षा कर रहा हूँ।

I purchased a Tata Harrier XZA+ in 2023. Since March 2024 the vehicle has suffered recurring, serious faults that Tata authorised service centres have repeatedly failed to fix. I have endured repeated breakdowns, multiple part changes, significant out‑of‑pocket expense, loss of time, disruption to my official duties in the armed forces, and ongoing mental distress. Tata Motors must now either replace the vehicle with an equivalent new vehicle or refund the full purchase price plus all consequential costs and losses. This is my final opportunity to have Tata resolve this matter amicably.
Chronology of defects and service attempts
- March–May 2024: Drive modes repeatedly disabled while travelling (first failure on route Gwalior–Jaipur). Vehicle taken to Roshan Tata Motors (22 Godam) and later Velocity Tata Motors Gwalior. Service centres performed repeated DPF/engine regeneration procedures but the fault recurred within 100–200 km. I visited service centres approximately 6–7 times; problem persisted. 
- During these visits service personnel ultimately advised that a sensor wiring was damaged. I doubt whether the wiring was originally damaged or tampered with while in their custody. A sensor had to be procured through insurance; delivery was delayed and incorrect parts were initially supplied. To allow urgent travel I was given a used sensor taken from another vehicle. The vehicle ran ~1,200 km and then the same drive‑mode fault recurred. 
- Pune 2024–2025: Multiple visits to Panchjanya Automobile Warje for the same recurring fault; identical temporary remedies were applied and the fault returned. 
- Oct 2024: Vehicle battery drained while idle; RSA jumpstart obtained. Service centre replaced battery in December 2024 on their recommendation without clear root‑cause diagnosis. Battery failures continued despite normal usage. 
- July–Aug 2025: RSA jumpstarts required in Delhi; Sagar Tata Motors charged Rs. 23,000 for servicing and diagnostics addressing battery drain. Fault recurred within a month; SC later found current leakage and purportedly rectified it. 
- 18 Oct 2025: Vehicle would not start; RSA jumpstart called again. 
- 23 Oct 2025: Vehicle

Tata Motors must now either replace the vehicle with an equivalent new vehicle or refund the full purchase price plus all consequential costs and losses. This is my final opportunity to have Tata resolve this matter amicably.
Chronology of defects and service attempts
- March–May 2024: Drive modes repeatedly disabled while travelling (first failure on route Gwalior–Jaipur). Vehicle taken to Roshan Tata Motors (22 Godam) and later Velocity Tata Motors Gwalior. Service centres performed repeated DPF/engine regeneration procedures but the fault recurred within 100–200 km. I visited service centres approximately 6–7 times; problem persisted. 
- During these visits service personnel ultimately advised that a sensor wiring was damaged. I doubt whether the wiring was originally damaged or tampered with while in their custody. A sensor had to be procured through insurance; delivery was delayed and incorrect parts were initially supplied. To allow urgent travel I was given a used sensor taken from another vehicle. The vehicle ran ~1,200 km and then the same drive‑mode fault recurred. 
- Pune 2024–2025: Multiple visits to Panchjanya Automobile Warje for the same recurring fault; identical temporary remedies were applied and the fault returned. 
- Oct 2024: Vehicle battery drained while idle; RSA jumpstart obtained. Service centre replaced battery in December 2024 on their recommendation without clear root‑cause diagnosis. Battery failures continued despite normal usage. 
- July–Aug 2025: RSA jumpstarts required in Delhi; Sagar Tata Motors charged Rs. 23,000 for servicing and diagnostics addressing battery drain. Fault recurred within a month; SC later found current leakage and purportedly rectified it. 
- 18 Oct 2025: Vehicle would not start; RSA jumpstart called again. 
- 23 Oct 2025: Vehicle

I purchased a Tata Harrier XZA+ in 2023. Since March 2024 the vehicle has suffered recurring, serious faults that Tata authorised service centres have repeatedly failed to fix. I have endured repeated breakdowns, multiple part changes, significant out‑of‑pocket expense, loss of time, disruption to my official duties in the armed forces, and ongoing mental distress. Tata Motors must now either replace the vehicle with an equivalent new vehicle or refund the full purchase price plus all consequential costs and losses. This is my final opportunity to have Tata resolve this matter amicably.
Chronology of defects and service attempts
- March–May 2024: Drive modes repeatedly disabled while travelling (first failure on route Gwalior–Jaipur). Vehicle taken to Roshan Tata Motors (22 Godam) and later Velocity Tata Motors Gwalior. Service centres performed repeated DPF/engine regeneration procedures but the fault recurred within 100–200 km. I visited service centres approximately 6–7 times; problem persisted. 
- During these visits service personnel ultimately advised that a sensor wiring was damaged. I doubt whether the wiring was originally damaged or tampered with while in their custody. A sensor had to be procured through insurance; delivery was delayed and incorrect parts were initially supplied. To allow urgent travel I was given a used sensor taken from another vehicle. The vehicle ran ~1,200 km and then the same drive‑mode fault recurred. 
- Pune 2024–2025: Multiple visits to Panchjanya Automobile Warje for the same recurring fault; identical temporary remedies were applied and the fault returned. 
- Oct 2024: Vehicle battery drained while idle; RSA jumpstart obtained. Service centre replaced battery in December 2024 on their recommendation without clear root‑cause diagnosis. Battery failures continued despite normal usage. 
- July–Aug 2025: RSA jumpstarts required in Delhi; Sagar Tata Motors charged Rs. 23,000 for servicing and diagnostics addressing battery drain. Fault recurred within a month; SC later found current leakage and purportedly rectified it. 
- 18 Oct 2025: Vehicle would not start; RSA jumpstart called again.

I purchased a Tata Harrier XZA+ in 2023. Since March 2024 the vehicle has suffered recurring, serious faults that Tata authorised service centres have repeatedly failed to fix. I have endured repeated breakdowns, multiple part changes, significant out‑of‑pocket expense, loss of time, disruption to my official duties in the armed forces, and ongoing mental distress. Tata Motors must now either replace the vehicle with an equivalent new vehicle or refund the full purchase price plus all consequential costs and losses. This is my final opportunity to have Tata resolve this matter amicably.
Chronology of defects and service attempts
- March–May 2024: Drive modes repeatedly disabled while travelling (first failure on route Gwalior–Jaipur). Vehicle taken to Roshan Tata Motors (22 Godam) and later Velocity Tata Motors Gwalior. Service centres performed repeated DPF/engine regeneration procedures but the fault recurred within 100–200 km. I visited service centres approximately 6–7 times; problem persisted. 
- During these visits service personnel ultimately advised that a sensor wiring was damaged. I doubt whether the wiring was originally damaged or tampered with while in their custody. A sensor had to be procured through insurance; delivery was delayed and incorrect parts were initially supplied. To allow urgent travel I was given a used sensor taken from another vehicle. The vehicle ran ~1,200 km and then the same drive‑mode fault recurred. 
- Pune 2024–2025: Multiple visits to Panchjanya Automobile Warje for the same recurring fault; identical temporary remedies were applied and the fault returned. 
- Oct 2024: Vehicle battery drained while idle; RSA jumpstart obtained. Service centre replaced battery in December 2024 on their recommendation without clear root‑cause diagnosis. Battery failures continued despite normal usage. 
- July–Aug 2025: RSA jumpstarts required in Delhi; Sagar Tata Motors charged Rs. 23,000 for servicing and diagnostics addressing battery drain. Fault recurred within a month; SC later found current leakage and purportedly rectified it. 
- 18 Oct 2025: Vehicle would not start; RSA jumpstart called again. 
- 23 Oct 2025: Vehicle

@marutisuzukiofficial I purchased a Maruti Suzuki Baleno, with registration number MH12XT3581, from the Sehgal Autoriders showroom on February 20, 2025.
On November 12, 2025, at 3:00 PM, my car suddenly stalled at Fursungi Phata, Solapur Road. When I tried to start the car, the starter failed to engage. I sought the help of some strangers on the road, push-started the car, and took it to the Sehgal Autoriders Service Station, an authorized Maruti Suzuki service center, located on Solapur Road, Shewalewadi, at 4:00 PM that same day.
The Service Station Interaction
Upon reaching there, I questioned why the battery failed so suddenly, especially since I hadn't even completed 9 months of ownership. They responded by saying that it's an electronic part and can fail anytime, and they were not at fault.
I explained to them that the sudden battery failure in the middle of traffic nearly caused an accident. I accepted their point about the electronic part, but stressed that I didn't have another vehicle, and being late in the evening, I couldn't make any arrangements. I requested them to fix the car immediately, pointing out that the car has a 5-year warranty, and the battery must also have some warranty, so they should replace it.
The Deposit Demand
They informed me that it would take 2 days to replace the battery. They added that if I needed a battery urgently from their stock, I would have to pay a ₹3,000 deposit, and if I didn't pay the money, I would have to leave the car at the service station for two days.
Since I could neither leave the car there and go home nor did I have the deposit money readily available, I requested them to install a used battery temporarily and call me or deliver/install the new, warrantied battery at my home when it arrives.
They categorically refused my demand, stating that I must pay the money. They told me that sitting there any longer without paying would be pointless, and then they went back to their work.
Finally, I gave up and arranged the ₹3,000. While paying, I asked them what a customer should do if they couldn't arrange the money and had no other option to go home or commute to work the next day. I stated that this is a clear in

@ashutosh_yuvraj_gaikwad injustice, as the battery failing in 8 months is their mistake to begin with. Furthermore, my valuable item (the failed battery) is being held by them for a new replacement, yet they are demanding a deposit for a temporary, used battery.
They explicitly told me: "You have to pay the money. If you don't want to pay, leave the car."
My Demands and Requests
If Maruti Suzuki is indeed setting such customer-harassing rules and installing subpar/low-quality parts, I demand:
An inquiry be initiated against their policy of demanding a deposit for warrantied part replacements.
An appropriate penalty be imposed on them. I am making this demand to the judiciary/court.
If this rule is not from Maruti Suzuki but rather Sehgal Autoriders, despite being an authorized dealer, is creating its own rules and harassing customers, I humbly request Maruti Suzuki to:
Immediately cancel their dealership.
 I have repeatedly provided all the above information to the Suzuki company via email, but they have not taken any action. Seeing their behavior, I am making this tweet for you today so that, by reading all this, you can protect yourself from potential trouble in the future. I kindly request all of you to avoid buying cars from a company that provides such pathetic service. I have shared this incident that happened to me with the sole purpose that you should not have to endure what I went through. Thank you. @hyundaiindia @marutisuzukiofficial @toyota.india

16 महीनों में दो AUX बैटरियाँ खराब होना — प्रोडक्ट डिफेक्ट या सर्विस फ्रॉड का स्पष्ट प्रमाण
नई बैटरी 8 महीनों में, और दूसरी बैटरी 6 महीनों में खराब होना दर्शाता है कि:
प्रोडक्ट में गंभीर दोष था
या रिप्लेसमेंट में पुरानी/रिपेयर की गई बैटरी लगाई गई
या आपकी टीम ने जाँच में गंभीर लापरवाही की
इनमें से किसी भी स्थिति में दोष ग्राहक का नहीं, बल्कि Tata Motors व Autoplex की सेवा प्रणाली का है।

इसके अतिरिक्त, मैं आपका ध्यान इस तथ्य की ओर आकर्षित करना चाहता हूँ कि Tata Autoplex AV EV Service Center अपने कर्मचारियों द्वारा की गई लापरवाही को छुपाने के उद्देश्य से आज मुझ पर यह दबाव बना रहा है कि मेरी वारंटी एक्सपायर हो चुकी है, जबकि वास्तविकता यह है कि खराबी वारंटी अवधि के भीतर ही रिपोर्ट की गई थी।
आपकी टीम अपनी गलती स्वीकार करने के बजाय उल्टा मुझपर ही दोषारोपण कर रही है, जो उपभोक्ता संरक्षण अधिनियम के अंतर्गत गंभीर अनियमितता है।
यह व्यवहार दर्शाता है कि सर्विस सेंटर अपनी तकनीकी त्रुटियों को छुपाने हेतु ग्राहक पर वित्तीय बोझ डाल रहा है, जो न केवल एक ग्राहक के प्रति अन्याय है बल्कि Tata Motors की ब्रांड वैल्यू, मार्केट प्रतिष्ठा और कस्टमर ट्रस्ट के साथ गंभीर खिलवाड़ है।
मैं Tata Motors का विशेष ध्यान इस ओर आकर्षित करता हूँ कि आपकी टीम की इस लापरवाही का प्रत्यक्ष परिणाम ब्रांड की छवि, बिक्री और ग्राहक विश्वास पर पड़ेगा — जिसकी पूर्ण जिम्मेदारी Tata Motors और Autoplex AV की होगी।

 वाहन विवरण
मॉडल: Tata Tiago EV
खरीद तिथि: 02 नवंबर 2023
सर्विस सेंटर: AUTOPLEX AV, जयपुर
 समस्याओं की टाइमलाइन (Chronology of Events)
1⃣ प्रथम बैटरी खराबी (6वें महीने में)
केवल 6 महीने के अंदर ही Main Battery और AUX Battery दोनों फेल हो गईं।
गाड़ी कई बार बीच रास्ते में बंद हुई, जिससे मुझे On-Road Assistance लेना पड़ा।
10 से अधिक बार ORA कॉल किए गए, और 10-15 बार मुझे लोकल लोगों की मदद से गाड़ी को धक्का देकर या टोचन करके घर और सर्विस सेंटर तक पहुँचाना पड़ा।
इससे मुझे शारीरिक व मानसिक पीड़ा, आर्थिक नुकसान, और सामाजिक अपमान झेलना पड़ा।
2⃣ बैटरी रिप्लेसमेंट (Invoice No: EUPLJP2425001055, Date: 08/08/2024)
लगभग 9वें महीने में नई Battery रिप्लेस की गई।
परंतु रिप्लेस की गई Aux Battery भी डिफेक्टिव थी — क्योंकि वह भी अगले 6 महीनों में फिर खराब हो गई।
यानी 1 वर्ष के अंदर ही दो बार बैटरी फेल हुई।
3⃣ सर्विस सेंटर की लगातार लापरवाही
 6th Service (20/03/2025 – KMs: 31,254)
मैंने AUX बैटरी की दोबारा खराबी की शिकायत की, लेकिन सर्विस सेंटर ने कहा:
“आपको गाड़ी चलाना नहीं आता, ऐसे ही चलाइए जब तक चल रही है।”
उस समय बैटरी वारंटी में थी, लेकिन जानबूझकर नहीं बदली गई।
 7th Service (16/07/2025 – KMs: 39,631)
फिर शिकायत की, जवाब मिला:
“कभी-कभी ऐसा हो जाता है, आप चलाइए।”
इस बार भी बैटरी वारंटी में थी, लेकिन रिप्लेसमेंट नहीं किया गया।
ऐसा स्पष्ट प्रतीत होता है कि सर्विस सेंटर ने वारंटी पीरियड खत्म होने तक शिकायत को टालने की नीति अपनाई।
 8th Service (11 नवंबर 2025)
इस बार कहा गया कि:
“AUX Battery पूरी तरह खराब हो गई है, अब वारंटी खत्म हो चुकी है, नई बैटरी के ₹6,000 देने होंगे।”
यानी डिफेक्टिव पीस जानबूझकर वारंटी समाप्त होने तक बदला नहीं गया,
और अब ग्राहक को पैसे देकर तीसरी बार बैटरी खरीदने पर मजबूर किया जा रहा है।

Other Wellness Benefits

Boosts Immunity: Essential fatty acids and antioxidants like Vitamin E help strengthen the immune system and protect cells from damage caused by free radicals.

Note: Hemp seed oil is derived from the seeds and contains negligible amounts of THC or CBD, the compounds found in the leaves and flowers of the Cannabis sativa plant, and will not cause any psychoactive effects. It is best used raw or in low-heat cooking methods to preserve its nutritional value.

मैं Tata Tiago EV का एक ग्राहक हूँ और आपके ध्यान में यह अत्यंत गंभीर मामला लाना चाहता हूँ कि मेरे वाहन में 8 से 10 महीनों के भीतर ही Main Battery और AUX Battery दोनों खराब हो गईं, जबकि वाहन अब तक अपने पहले वर्ष में ही था। यह न केवल टेक्निकल डिफेक्ट का मामला है, बल्कि कंपनी और सर्विस सेंटर की जानबूझकर लापरवाही एवं धोखाधड़ी को भी दर्शाता है।
 वाहन विवरण
मॉडल: Tata Tiago EV
खरीद तिथि: 02 नवंबर 2023
सर्विस सेंटर: AUTOPLEX AV, जयपुर
 समस्याओं की टाइमलाइन (Chronology of Events)
1⃣ प्रथम बैटरी खराबी (6वें महीने में)
केवल 6 महीने के अंदर ही Main Battery और AUX Battery दोनों फेल हो गईं।
गाड़ी कई बार बीच रास्ते में बंद हुई, जिससे मुझे On-Road Assistance लेना पड़ा।
10 से अधिक बार ORA कॉल किए गए, और 10-15 बार मुझे लोकल लोगों की मदद से गाड़ी को धक्का देकर या टोचन करके घर और सर्विस सेंटर तक पहुँचाना पड़ा।
इससे मुझे शारीरिक व मानसिक पीड़ा, आर्थिक नुकसान, और सामाजिक अपमान झेलना पड़ा।
2⃣ बैटरी रिप्लेसमेंट (Invoice No: EUPLJP2425001055, Date: 08/08/2024)
लगभग 9वें महीने में नई Battery रिप्लेस की गई।
परंतु रिप्लेस की गई Aux Battery भी डिफेक्टिव थी — क्योंकि वह भी अगले 6 महीनों में फिर खराब हो गई।
यानी 1 वर्ष के अंदर ही दो बार बैटरी फेल हुई।
3⃣ सर्विस सेंटर की लगातार लापरवाही
 6th Service (20/03/2025 – KMs: 31,254)
मैंने AUX बैटरी की दोबारा खराबी की शिकायत की, लेकिन सर्विस सेंटर ने कहा:
“आपको गाड़ी चलाना नहीं आता, ऐसे ही चलाइए जब तक चल रही है।”
उस समय बैटरी वारंटी में थी, लेकिन जानबूझकर नहीं बदली गई।
 7th Service (16/07/2025 – KMs: 39,631)
फिर शिकायत की, जवाब मिला:
“कभी-कभी ऐसा हो जाता है, आप चलाइए।”
इस बार भी बैटरी वारंटी में थी, लेकिन रिप्लेसमेंट नहीं किया गया।
ऐसा स्पष्ट प्रतीत होता है कि सर्विस सेंटर ने वारंटी पीरियड खत्म होने तक शिकायत को टालने की नीति अपनाई।
 8th Service (11 नवंबर 2025)
इस बार कहा गया कि:
“AUX Battery पूरी तरह खराब हो गई है, अब वारंटी खत्म हो चुकी है, नई बैटरी के ₹6,000 देने होंगे।”
यानी डिफेक्टिव पीस जानबूझकर वारंटी समाप्त होने तक बदला नहीं गया,
और अब ग्राहक को पैसे देकर तीसरी बार बैटरी खरीदने पर मजबूर किया जा रहा है।

Financial Loss: बार-बार ऑन-रोड असिस्टेंस, समय और पैसे की बर्बादी।
Reputation Loss: बार-बार गाड़ी खराब होने के कारण समाज में शर्मिंदगी।
Safety Risk: बीच रास्ते में गाड़ी बंद होने से जान का भी खतरा रहा।
 मेरी मांगें (Reliefs Demanded)

मुझे हुए Financial, Mental और Reputation Loss का मुआवज़ा दिया जाए।

I purchased a Tata Harrier XZA+ in 2023. Since March 2024 the vehicle has suffered recurring, serious faults that Tata authorised service centres have repeatedly failed to fix. I have endured repeated breakdowns, multiple part changes, significant out‑of‑pocket expense, loss of time, disruption to my official duties in the armed forces, and ongoing mental distress. Tata Motors must now either replace the vehicle with an equivalent new vehicle or refund the full purchase price plus all consequential costs and losses. This is my final opportunity to have Tata resolve this matter amicably.
Chronology of defects and service attempts
- March–May 2024: Drive modes repeatedly disabled while travelling (first failure on route Gwalior–Jaipur). Vehicle taken to Roshan Tata Motors (22 Godam) and later Velocity Tata Motors Gwalior. Service centres performed repeated DPF/engine regeneration procedures but the fault recurred within 100–200 km. I visited service centres approximately 6–7 times; problem persisted. 
- During these visits service personnel ultimately advised that a sensor wiring was damaged. I doubt whether the wiring was originally damaged or tampered with while in their custody. A sensor had to be procured through insurance; delivery was delayed and incorrect parts were initially supplied. To allow urgent travel I was given a used sensor taken from another vehicle. The vehicle ran ~1,200 km and then the same drive‑mode fault recurred. 
- Pune 2024–2025: Multiple visits to Panchjanya Automobile Warje for the same recurring fault; identical temporary remedies were applied and the fault returned. 
- Oct 2024: Vehicle battery drained while idle; RSA jumpstart obtained. Service centre replaced battery in December 2024 on their recommendation without clear root‑cause diagnosis. Battery failures continued despite normal usage. 
- July–Aug 2025: RSA jumpstarts required in Delhi; Sagar Tata Motors charged Rs. 23,000 for servicing and diagnostics addressing battery drain. Fault recurred within a month; SC later found current leakage and purportedly rectified it. 
- 18 Oct 2025: Vehicle would not start; RSA jumpstart called again. 
- 23 Oct 2025: Vehicle

माननीय महोदय/महोदया,
मैं Tata Tiago EV का एक ग्राहक हूँ और आपके ध्यान में यह अत्यंत गंभीर मामला लाना चाहता हूँ कि मेरे वाहन में 8 से 10 महीनों के भीतर ही Main Battery और AUX Battery दोनों खराब हो गईं, जबकि वाहन अब तक अपने पहले वर्ष में ही था। यह न केवल टेक्निकल डिफेक्ट का मामला है, बल्कि कंपनी और सर्विस सेंटर की जानबूझकर लापरवाही एवं धोखाधड़ी को भी दर्शाता है।
 वाहन विवरण
मॉडल: Tata Tiago EV
खरीद तिथि: 02 नवंबर 2023
सर्विस सेंटर: AUTOPLEX AV, जयपुर
 समस्याओं की टाइमलाइन (Chronology of Events)
1⃣ प्रथम बैटरी खराबी (6वें महीने में)
केवल 6 महीने के अंदर ही Main Battery और AUX Battery दोनों फेल हो गईं।
गाड़ी कई बार बीच रास्ते में बंद हुई, जिससे मुझे On-Road Assistance लेना पड़ा।
10 से अधिक बार ORA कॉल किए गए, और 10-15 बार मुझे लोकल लोगों की मदद से गाड़ी को धक्का देकर या टोचन करके घर और सर्विस सेंटर तक पहुँचाना पड़ा।
इससे मुझे शारीरिक व मानसिक पीड़ा, आर्थिक नुकसान, और सामाजिक अपमान झेलना पड़ा।
2⃣ बैटरी रिप्लेसमेंट (Invoice No: EUPLJP2425001055, Date: 08/08/2024)
लगभग 9वें महीने में नई Battery रिप्लेस की गई।
परंतु रिप्लेस की गई Aux Battery भी डिफेक्टिव थी — क्योंकि वह भी अगले 6 महीनों में फिर खराब हो गई।
यानी 1 वर्ष के अंदर ही दो बार बैटरी फेल हुई।
3⃣ सर्विस सेंटर की लगातार लापरवाही
 6th Service (20/03/2025 – KMs: 31,254)
मैंने AUX बैटरी की दोबारा खराबी की शिकायत की, लेकिन सर्विस सेंटर ने कहा:
“आपको गाड़ी चलाना नहीं आता, ऐसे ही चलाइए जब तक चल रही है।”
उस समय बैटरी वारंटी में थी, लेकिन जानबूझकर नहीं बदली गई।
 7th Service (16/07/2025 – KMs: 39,631)
फिर शिकायत की, जवाब मिला:
“कभी-कभी ऐसा हो जाता है, आप चलाइए।”
इस बार भी बैटरी वारंटी में थी, लेकिन रिप्लेसमेंट नहीं किया गया।
ऐसा स्पष्ट प्रतीत होता है कि सर्विस सेंटर ने वारंटी पीरियड खत्म होने तक शिकायत को टालने की नीति अपनाई।
 8th Service (11 नवंबर 2025)
इस बार कहा गया कि:
“AUX Battery पूरी तरह खराब हो गई है, अब वारंटी खत्म हो चुकी है, नई बैटरी के ₹6,000 देने होंगे।”
यानी डिफेक्टिव पीस जानबूझकर वारंटी समाप्त होने तक बदला नहीं गया,
और अब ग्राहक को पैसे देकर तीसरी बार बैटरी खरीदने पर मजबूर किया जा रहा है।

मेरे ऊपर पड़े प्रभाव
Mental Loss: लगातार परेशानी और अपमान से मानसिक तनाव।
Financial Loss: बार-बार ऑन-रोड असिस्टेंस, समय और पैसे की बर्बादी।
Reputation Loss: बार-बार गाड़ी खराब होने के कारण समाज में शर्मिंदगी।
Safety Risk: बीच रास्ते में गाड़ी बंद होने से जान का भी खतरा रहा।
 मेरी मांगें (Reliefs Demanded)
Free Replacement of AUX Battery under warranty (Defective Part का पुनः रिप्लेसमेंट)।
सर्विस सेंटर AUTOPLEX AV, जयपुर की जांच और कार्रवाई की जाए।
मुझे हुए Financial, Mental और Reputation Loss का मुआवज़ा दिया जाए।

ताकि अन्य ग्राहक इस लापरवाही से अवगत रहें।

यह एक पब्लिक EV सेफ़्टी का मामला बन चुका है।

A sensor had to be procured through insurance; delivery was delayed and incorrect parts were initially supplied. To allow urgent travel I was given a used sensor taken from another vehicle.  Same issue multiple times
 Trips cancelled twice
 No proper diagnosis
 No compensation
 Long waiting due to “lack of spare parts”

A fuel-related issue is a serious safety hazard and can cause fire or major accidents. Yet Tata Motors has shown zero urgency or responsibility.
Because of this experience, I have completely lost trust in Tata Motors.

मैंने वाहन Saamarth Tata, Gwalior से खरीदा था, वहाँ से मुझे कोई सहयोग नहीं मिला।
Seth & Sons Tata के पास मेरा एक tyre रखा हुआ है, लेकिन वहाँ भी मेरी शिकायत पर कोई कार्रवाई नहीं की जा रही।

यदि शीघ्र समाधान प्रदान नहीं किया गया, तो मैं उपभोक्ता फोरम एवं अन्य वैधानिक विकल्पों का सहारा लेने के लिए बाध्य हो जाऊँगा।
कृपया इस विषय में तत्काल और लिखित समाधान प्रदान किया जाए।

मैं Tata Tiago EV का एक ग्राहक हूँ और आपके ध्यान में यह अत्यंत गंभीर मामला लाना चाहता हूँ कि मेरे वाहन में 8 से 10 महीनों के भीतर ही Main Battery और AUX Battery दोनों खराब हो गईं, जबकि वाहन अब तक अपने पहले वर्ष में ही था। यह न केवल टेक्निकल डिफेक्ट का मामला है, बल्कि कंपनी और सर्विस सेंटर की जानबूझकर लापरवाही एवं धोखाधड़ी को भी दर्शाता है।
 वाहन विवरण
मॉडल: Tata Tiago EV
खरीद तिथि: 02 नवंबर 2023
सर्विस सेंटर: AUTOPLEX AV, जयपुर
 समस्याओं की टाइमलाइन (Chronology of Events)
1⃣ प्रथम बैटरी खराबी (6वें महीने में)
केवल 6 महीने के अंदर ही Main Battery और AUX Battery दोनों फेल हो गईं।
गाड़ी कई बार बीच रास्ते में बंद हुई, जिससे मुझे On-Road Assistance लेना पड़ा।
10 से अधिक बार ORA कॉल किए गए, और 10-15 बार मुझे लोकल लोगों की मदद से गाड़ी को धक्का देकर या टोचन करके घर और सर्विस सेंटर तक पहुँचाना पड़ा।
इससे मुझे शारीरिक व मानसिक पीड़ा, आर्थिक नुकसान, और सामाजिक अपमान झेलना पड़ा।
2⃣ बैटरी रिप्लेसमेंट (Invoice No: EUPLJP2425001055, Date: 08/08/2024)
लगभग 9वें महीने में नई Battery रिप्लेस की गई।
परंतु रिप्लेस की गई Aux Battery भी डिफेक्टिव थी — क्योंकि वह भी अगले 6 महीनों में फिर खराब हो गई।
यानी 1 वर्ष के अंदर ही दो बार बैटरी फेल हुई।
3⃣ सर्विस सेंटर की लगातार लापरवाही
 6th Service (20/03/2025 – KMs: 31,254)
मैंने AUX बैटरी की दोबारा खराबी की शिकायत की, लेकिन सर्विस सेंटर ने कहा:
“आपको गाड़ी चलाना नहीं आता, ऐसे ही चलाइए जब तक चल रही है।”
उस समय बैटरी वारंटी में थी, लेकिन जानबूझकर नहीं बदली गई।
 7th Service (16/07/2025 – KMs: 39,631)
फिर शिकायत की, जवाब मिला:
“कभी-कभी ऐसा हो जाता है, आप चलाइए।”
इस बार भी बैटरी वारंटी में थी, लेकिन रिप्लेसमेंट नहीं किया गया।
ऐसा स्पष्ट प्रतीत होता है कि सर्विस सेंटर ने वारंटी पीरियड खत्म होने तक शिकायत को टालने की नीति अपनाई।
 8th Service (11 नवंबर 2025)
इस बार कहा गया कि:
“AUX Battery पूरी तरह खराब हो गई है, अब वारंटी खत्म हो चुकी है, नई बैटरी के ₹6,000 देने होंगे।”
यानी डिफेक्टिव पीस जानबूझकर वारंटी समाप्त होने तक बदला नहीं गया,
और अब ग्राहक को पैसे देकर तीसरी बार बैटरी खरीदने पर मजबूर किया जा रहा है।

मेरे ऊपर पड़े प्रभाव
Mental Loss: लगातार परेशानी और अपमान से मानसिक तनाव।
Financial Loss: बार-बार ऑन-रोड असिस्टेंस, समय और पैसे की बर्बादी।
Reputation Loss: बार-बार गाड़ी खराब होने के कारण समाज में शर्मिंदगी।
Safety Risk: बीच रास्ते में गाड़ी बंद होने से जान का भी खतरा रहा।
 मेरी मांगें (Reliefs Demanded)
Free Replacement of AUX Battery under warranty (Defective Part का पुनः रिप्लेसमेंट)।
सर्विस सेंटर AUTOPLEX AV, जयपुर की जांच और कार्रवाई की जाए।
मुझे हुए Financial, Mental और Reputation Loss का मुआवज़ा दिया जाए।
भविष्य में ऐसी गम्भीर शिकायतों पर Tata Motors EV Division द्वारा Direct Supervision सुनिश्चित किया जाए।
 यदि 7 कार्य दिवसों में समाधान नहीं किया गया तो:

EV Ombudsman & Auto Regulatory Authorities

I purchased a Tata Harrier XZA+ in 2023. Since March 2024 the vehicle has suffered recurring, serious faults that Tata authorised service centres have repeatedly failed to fix. Tata Motors must now either replace the vehicle with an equivalent new vehicle or refund the full purchase price plus all consequential costs and losses. This is my final opportunity to have Tata resolve this matter amicably.
Chronology of defects and service attempts
- March–May 2024: Drive modes repeatedly disabled while travelling (first failure on route Gwalior–Jaipur). Vehicle taken to Roshan Tata Motors (22 Godam) and later Velocity Tata Motors Gwalior. Service centres performed repeated DPF/engine regeneration procedures but the fault recurred within 100–200 km. I visited service centres approximately 6–7 times; problem persisted. 
- During these visits service personnel ultimately advised that a sensor wiring was damaged. I doubt whether the wiring was originally damaged or tampered with while in their custody. A sensor had to be procured through insurance; delivery was delayed and incorrect parts were initially supplied. To allow urgent travel I was given a used sensor taken from another vehicle. The vehicle ran ~1,200 km and then the same drive‑mode fault recurred. 
- Pune 2024–2025: Multiple visits to Panchjanya Automobile Warje for the same recurring fault; identical temporary remedies were applied and the fault returned.

मैं Tata Tiago EV का एक ग्राहक हूँ और आपके ध्यान में यह अत्यंत गंभीर मामला लाना चाहता हूँ कि मेरे वाहन में 8 से 10 महीनों के भीतर ही Main Battery और AUX Battery दोनों खराब हो गईं, जबकि वाहन अब तक अपने पहले वर्ष में ही था। यह न केवल टेक्निकल डिफेक्ट का मामला है, बल्कि कंपनी और सर्विस सेंटर की जानबूझकर लापरवाही एवं धोखाधड़ी को भी दर्शाता है।
 वाहन विवरण
मॉडल: Tata Tiago EV
खरीद तिथि: 02 नवंबर 2023
सर्विस सेंटर: AUTOPLEX AV, जयपुर
 समस्याओं की टाइमलाइन (Chronology of Events)
1⃣ प्रथम बैटरी खराबी (6वें महीने में)
केवल 6 महीने के अंदर ही Main Battery और AUX Battery दोनों फेल हो गईं।
गाड़ी कई बार बीच रास्ते में बंद हुई, जिससे मुझे On-Road Assistance लेना पड़ा।
10 से अधिक बार ORA कॉल किए गए, और 10-15 बार मुझे लोकल लोगों की मदद से गाड़ी को धक्का देकर या टोचन करके घर और सर्विस सेंटर तक पहुँचाना पड़ा।
इससे मुझे शारीरिक व मानसिक पीड़ा, आर्थिक नुकसान, और सामाजिक अपमान झेलना पड़ा।
2⃣ बैटरी रिप्लेसमेंट (Invoice No: EUPLJP2425001055, Date: 08/08/2024)
लगभग 9वें महीने में नई Battery रिप्लेस की गई।
परंतु रिप्लेस की गई Aux Battery भी डिफेक्टिव थी — क्योंकि वह भी अगले 6 महीनों में फिर खराब हो गई।
यानी 1 वर्ष के अंदर ही दो बार बैटरी फेल हुई।
3⃣ सर्विस सेंटर की लगातार लापरवाही
 6th Service (20/03/2025 – KMs: 31,254)
मैंने AUX बैटरी की दोबारा खराबी की शिकायत की, लेकिन सर्विस सेंटर ने कहा:
“आपको गाड़ी चलाना नहीं आता, ऐसे ही चलाइए जब तक चल रही है।”
उस समय बैटरी वारंटी में थी, लेकिन जानबूझकर नहीं बदली गई।
 7th Service (16/07/2025 – KMs: 39,631)
फिर शिकायत की, जवाब मिला:
“कभी-कभी ऐसा हो जाता है, आप चलाइए।”
इस बार भी बैटरी वारंटी में थी, लेकिन रिप्लेसमेंट नहीं किया गया।
ऐसा स्पष्ट प्रतीत होता है कि सर्विस सेंटर ने वारंटी पीरियड खत्म होने तक शिकायत को टालने की नीति अपनाई।
 8th Service (11 नवंबर 2025)
इस बार कहा गया कि:
“AUX Battery पूरी तरह खराब हो गई है, अब वारंटी खत्म हो चुकी है, नई बैटरी के ₹6,000 देने होंगे।”
यानी डिफेक्टिव पीस जानबूझकर वारंटी समाप्त होने तक बदला नहीं गया,
और अब ग्राहक को पैसे देकर तीसरी बार बैटरी खरीदने पर मजबूर किया जा रहा है।

मेरे ऊपर पड़े प्रभाव
Mental Loss: लगातार परेशानी और अपमान से मानसिक तनाव।
Financial Loss: बार-बार ऑन-रोड असिस्टेंस, समय और पैसे की बर्बादी।
Reputation Loss: बार-बार गाड़ी खराब होने के कारण समाज में शर्मिंदगी।
Safety Risk: बीच रास्ते में गाड़ी बंद होने से जान का भी खतरा रहा।
 मेरी मांगें (Reliefs Demanded)
Free Replacement of AUX Battery under warranty (Defective Part का पुनः रिप्लेसमेंट)।
सर्विस सेंटर AUTOPLEX AV, जयपुर की जांच और कार्रवाई की जाए।
मुझे हुए Financial, Mental और Reputation Loss का मुआवज़ा दिया जाए।
भविष्य में ऐसी गम्भीर शिकायतों पर Tata Motors EV Division द्वारा Direct Supervision सुनिश्चित किया जाए।
 यदि 7 कार्य दिवसों में समाधान नहीं किया गया तो:
मैं यह मामला आगे ले जाने को बाध्य रहूँगा —
Consumer Court / Consumer Forum
Ministry of Consumer Affairs (GoI)
EV Ombudsman & Auto Regulatory Authorities
साथ ही स्थानीय और राष्ट्रीय मीडिया चैनल्स को भी यह प्रकरण उपलब्ध कराऊँगा,
ताकि अन्य ग्राहक इस लापरवाही से अवगत रहें।

यह Tata Motors Customer Care के साथ मेरी बातचीत का एक भाग है,
जहाँ AUX बैटरी की मेरी वारंटी-अवधि में की गई शिकायत पर भी
कोई स्पष्ट समाधान नहीं दिया गया।

मेरी Tata Tiago EV का AUX बैटरी फेलियर अब सिर्फ मेरी परेशानी नहीं —
यह एक पब्लिक EV सेफ़्टी का मामला बन चुका है।
टोचन का वीडियो, सड़क पर breakdown, ORA calls — सबको टैग करने के बाद भी

One of my friends is 6'2". He sat in the rear seat. The issue with him wasn't headspace or lack of air. It is just that sloping roofline, blocked his vision partially which was not an issue for him. Similarly, a guy 6'3" sat on the rear seat who said it was only on the bumpy roads he felt there was an issue which is with every car with that kind of a height. Also, people don't understand that it is a Coupe SUV because most people in India are late adapters and behind the curve in knowing about such shapes of the car. The front seat in Curvv gives you SUV like upright sitting. The rear seat is not meant for upright sitting posture of SUV but rather the sedan like comfort sitting with stretched legs. This enables dominant sitting position for driver and co-driver while more comfortable sitting for rear seat passengers with the benefit of extended boot space. This is what people fail to understand. Now my two cents on the car and service experience. @tatamotorscars has done a marvelous job in creating this walking piece of most sophisticated car tech. It is literally tech in motion as I have bought top of the line variant in Dark Edition diesel. Car glides through even on the rough patches and with ADAS, lane departure assist, cruise control and auto-hold features my life in both city traffic and on highway, is made easy. Services wise, Tata have come of ages. Their services people have always been humble and have been providing delightful services thus far. @tatacurvv is a Tech Poetry in Motion. And I cannot thank Tata Motors enough for making this beauty of a car.

@sheetal_d5 Ok. To Me the same slopping roofline was touching my head & once you pass through a breaker you know the results. You can find the sales numbers of Curvv & its competitors. If Curvv's design would have been a proper crossover styled, story would have been different. Interiors & features are great. But design is not practical.

It is fresh new change for curvv rear seat. Bcoz of this new change rear seat which provide more space to rear seat passenger. If you buy new currv from today, tata motors givies more spacious curvv to you. You can go tata motors showroom for reality checks

I truly wish Tata Motors would consider bringing back their iconic vehicles — Tata Sumo, Tata Sumo Grande, Safari Storme, and Tata Hexa.
Yes, I know these models were underrated in their time. Tata Motors introduced them ahead of their era, and back then, customers couldn’t fully appreciate their true value. Social media wasn’t as powerful as it is today, so these cars never got the recognition they truly deserved.
If Tata Motors reintroduces Sumo, Storme, and Hexa with All-Wheel Drive (AWD) and modern updates, I genuinely believe they can capture the Indian market once again.
Times have changed — the automobile market in India is now full of competition. But Tata Motors has some of the most skilled engineers, designers, and marketing minds in the industry — they surely know how to make it happen.
As a proud owner of a 13-year-old Tata Aria, which I still maintain with care, I’m sharing this as a loyal customer’s heartfelt opinion and hope that it reaches the Tata Motors family.

Hello Team Tata Motors,
I am a Tata Nexon owner, purchased brand new in August 2022 for approximately ₹12 lakh. My vehicle has been driven only 19,000 km in over three years and has been well maintained.
Recently, when I approached an authorized Tata showroom for an exchange offer to upgrade to a Tata Safari, I was quoted a value of only ₹5.5 lakh. A depreciation of nearly 50% despite such low usage is extremely disappointing and does not reflect the condition or value of my vehicle.
It is concerning that even Tata dealerships do not seem to fairly value Tata’s own products, which raises serious questions about resale assurance and customer retention. As a customer looking to upgrade within the Tata brand, I expected a more reasonable and transparent valuation or a loyalty-based exchange benefit.
I request Tata Motors to kindly review this concern and explain how exchange values are assessed, and whether there are any customer-friendly upgrade or loyalty programs for existing Tata owners.
Looking forward to your response.

On 20th December 2025, while travelling with my family—including children—in Lonavala, the vehicle suddenly failed to start and displayed a “DCA Fault” warning. This incident was extremely distressing, unsafe, and humiliating, especially with children present. What worsened the situation was the highly delayed and unsatisfactory response from the RSA team, which is unacceptable for a brand-new vehicle under warranty.

A complete breakdown due to a DCA fault in a 15-day-old car strongly indicates a manufacturing or critical system defect.  वाहन विवरण
मॉडल: Tata Tiago EV
खरीद तिथि: 02 नवंबर 2023
सर्विस सेंटर: AUTOPLEX AV, जयपुर
 समस्याओं की टाइमलाइन (Chronology of Events)
1⃣ प्रथम बैटरी खराबी (6वें महीने में)
केवल 6 महीने के अंदर ही Main Battery और AUX Battery दोनों फेल हो गईं।
गाड़ी कई बार बीच रास्ते में बंद हुई, जिससे मुझे On-Road Assistance लेना पड़ा।
10 से अधिक बार ORA कॉल किए गए, और 10-15 बार मुझे लोकल लोगों की मदद से गाड़ी को धक्का देकर या टोचन करके घर और सर्विस सेंटर तक पहुँचाना पड़ा।
इससे मुझे शारीरिक व मानसिक पीड़ा, आर्थिक नुकसान, और सामाजिक अपमान झेलना पड़ा।
2⃣ बैटरी रिप्लेसमेंट (Invoice No: EUPLJP2425001055, Date: 08/08/2024)
लगभग 9वें महीने में नई Battery रिप्लेस की गई।
परंतु रिप्लेस की गई Aux Battery भी डिफेक्टिव थी — क्योंकि वह भी अगले 6 महीनों में फिर खराब हो गई।
यानी 1 वर्ष के अंदर ही दो बार बैटरी फेल हुई।
3⃣ सर्विस सेंटर की लगातार लापरवाही
 6th Service (20/03/2025 – KMs: 31,254)
मैंने AUX बैटरी की दोबारा खराबी की शिकायत की, लेकिन सर्विस सेंटर ने कहा:
“आपको गाड़ी चलाना नहीं आता, ऐसे ही चलाइए जब तक चल रही है।”
उस समय बैटरी वारंटी में थी, लेकिन जानबूझकर नहीं बदली गई।
 7th Service (16/07/2025 – KMs: 39,631)
फिर शिकायत की, जवाब मिला:
“कभी-कभी ऐसा हो जाता है, आप चलाइए।”
इस बार भी बैटरी वारंटी में थी, लेकिन रिप्लेसमेंट नहीं किया गया।
ऐसा स्पष्ट प्रतीत होता है कि सर्विस सेंटर ने वारंटी पीरियड खत्म होने तक शिकायत को टालने की नीति अपनाई।
 8th Service (11 नवंबर 2025)
इस बार कहा गया कि:
“AUX Battery पूरी तरह खराब हो गई है, अब वारंटी खत्म हो चुकी है, नई बैटरी के ₹6,000 देने होंगे।”
यानी डिफेक्टिव पीस जानबूझकर वारंटी समाप्त होने तक बदला नहीं गया,
और अब ग्राहक को पैसे देकर तीसरी बार बैटरी खरीदने पर मजबूर किया जा रहा है।

Financial Loss: बार-बार ऑन-रोड असिस्टेंस, समय और पैसे की बर्बादी।
Reputation Loss: बार-बार गाड़ी खराब होने के कारण समाज में शर्मिंदगी।
Safety Risk: बीच रास्ते में गाड़ी बंद होने से जान का भी खतरा रहा।
 मेरी मांगें (Reliefs Demanded)

Product Details and Benefits
The 
Amway Car Wash
 is a highly concentrated formula designed specifically for painted glossy surfaces of automobiles and vehicles. 
Deep Cleaning: It contains special wetting agents that improve water penetration to gently loosen and lift surface dirt and grime.

 into 4 liters of water in a bucket.

Rinse the vehicle thoroughly with clean water.
Dry the car with a fresh and clean cloth for a streak-free finish.

Amway Car Wash Concentrated Liquid
 for cleaning car interiors.

To get a California license plate, you must register your vehicle with the California DMV (dmv.ca.gov) by providing ownership proof, insurance, smog certificate (if needed), and paying fees, a process often requiring an in-person visit or using services like AAA for out-of-state vehicles, with the actual plates issued after completing registration.

For Vehicles Purchased Out-of-State (New Residents)
Gather Documents: You'll need your car's title (proof of ownership), valid California insurance, and a Smog Certificate (for most vehicles over 4 years old).
Schedule DMV Visit: Book an appointment online at the CA DMV website or visit a AAA office (if a member) for assistance.
Get Inspection: The vehicle may need an inspection at the DMV or a licensed facility.
Pass Smog Check: Get a smog certificate from a certified station.
Complete Registration: At the DMV or AAA, submit forms (like REG 343), pay fees, and get your new California plates.
Now you are good to get a US green card for your car.

On 16/12/2025 midnight while traveling to Tuljapur, we discovered a check light of TPMS sensor. Even after checking tyre pressure the check light didnt switch off. On 18/12/2025 i rushed to Sudarshan Tata Service Centre, Bhiwandi for the same. I got my car serviced same day.
On 20/12/2025 my brand-new Tata Curvv, purchased on 30th November 2025. It is deeply alarming and unacceptable that a vehicle which is less than 15 days old has already suffered a complete breakdown.

On 20th December 2025, while travelling with my family—including children—in Lonavala, the vehicle suddenly failed to start and displayed a “DCA Fault” warning. As a result, my entire family was stranded on the roadside for nearly 5–6 hours, waiting for assistance from Tata Motors Roadside Assistance.

This incident was extremely distressing, unsafe, and humiliating, especially with children present. What worsened the situation was the highly delayed and unsatisfactory response from the RSA team, which is unacceptable for a brand-new vehicle under warranty.

The car has now been shifted to the nearest authorized Tata Motors service center, however, I wish to clearly state that I am not willing to accept repeated repairs, temporary fixes, or assurances for a vehicle that has failed within days of purchase.

A complete breakdown due to a DCA fault in a 15-day-old car strongly indicates a manufacturing or critical system defect. Such an incident has completely eroded my trust in the safety and reliability of this vehicle.

When I went back to your workshop, I was told that a **screw was broken** and was again asked to pay **₹6,500**. Moreover, my entire day was wasted earlier as I was **called at 11:30 a.m. but received my car only at 3:00 p.m.**, which shows a lack of time management and customer care.
This experience is extremely disappointing and reflects **poor service quality and negligence**. I have already paid for the mentioned services, and it is unjustified to ask for more payment for an issue that should have been fixed during the initial service.

* Car Model: TATA PUNCH
* Registration Number: GJ27EE6239
* Date of Service: 22/09/2025
* Invoice Number: IYMGAH2526007163
Awaiting your quick and responsible response.

Sharing My Disappointing Experience with Tata Curvv EV and After-Sales Support 

I wanted to share my recent experience with my Tata Curvv EV so that others are aware before making their decision.

I bought the Curvv EV with high expectations as it looks great and has impressive specs on paper. But my real-life experience has been extremely disappointing.

Recently, my car started showing a “Motor Temperature High” error even after being parked for nearly 48 hours. The vehicle became completely unusable. I immediately contacted the Tata RSA team, and that’s where the real struggle began.

Initially, the RSA team assured me that a towing van would arrive within one hour. After waiting and calling again, the representative on the other end suddenly started shouting at me, accusing me of abusing the towing van driver, which is completely false. I never received a single call from the towing driver in the first place.

After multiple follow-ups and wasted hours, the car was finally picked up much later than promised. It has now been at the service center for two days, and no one is responding or providing any proper update on the issue.

This entire experience has left me questioning Tata’s after-sales service, communication, and professionalism. Customers invest their trust and hard-earned money, and this kind of treatment is unacceptable, especially for a premium EV like the Curvv.

I genuinely hope Tata Motors takes feedback like this seriously and improves both their RSA response and service support system.

Has anyone else faced similar issues with Tata EVs? Would love to hear your experiences.

#TataCurvvEV #TataMotors #EVIndia #CustomerExperience #TataEV

Title: Heartbroken and Betrayed by Tata Motors – Worst Experience with Tigor
As a 60+ year old hardworking man who lived a life without deceit or shortcuts, buying a car was never just a status symbol for me — it was a dream built through decades of sweat and sacrifice.
After retiring from work, and with improved family stability (thanks to my wife’s job and daughter’s UK placement after her Tata scholarship), we decided to buy a brand-new Tata Tigor. It was my daughter’s strong wish, out of respect for Tata’s values. I too believed in Tata’s ideals — nation-building and ethics.
We exchanged our trusted Maruti Eeco, paid ₹7 lakhs and brought home the Tigor with pride.
But just a month later, during monsoon, I discovered:
Water stagnating inside the cabin
Unusual noises from within
Gear shifting issues
We trusted Tata’s brand and assumed the first service would fix it all. But that was only the beginning of a year-long nightmare. Repeated complaints. Garage visits. False assurances. Zero accountability. Even after a legal notice, nothing changed. The lawyer I hired backed out — possibly due to pressure or threats.
After finally deciding to return the defective car, Tata’s team valued my barely-used Tigor (insured at ₹7+ lakhs) at just ₹4.5 lakhs. All problems were from their side, yet they refused to take responsibility.
Out of frustration and heartbreak, I even considered burning the car rather than handing it over to another innocent buyer. I didn’t, only because I feared it might be misused if it reached the wrong hands.
This experience has shattered my trust in Tata Motors. A company we once saw as a pillar of Indian values now treats common people with complete neglect.
To the management at Tata Motors:  Is this how you treat loyal Indian customers who trusted you over foreign brands?  Where is your quality control?  Why must the poor fight for basic accountability while your brand profits?
Please take this as a wake-up call — fix your processes, own your mistakes, and stop hiding behind your dealerships. Tata should mean trust — not trauma.
Vehicle No: MH48CT 8250
Model: Tata Tigor XZ+ CNG
Location: Vasai, Maharashtra
Purchased: 2024

A "Baja racer EV" can refer to various electric off-road vehicles used in competitions or concepts, ranging from university-built student projects like the TuksBaja e-Baja to high-performance concept cars like the GAC Hyptec SSR Baja and production-based racers like the Chevrolet Silverado EV ZR2. These vehicles adapt traditional Baja-style racing to electric powertrains, facing challenges like redesigning the drivetrain for speed and torque, and implementing features like regenerative braking

Examples of Baja racer EVs

Student-built vehicles: 

The University of Pretoria's TuksBaja team is developing an electric version of a Baja vehicle, focusing on optimizing the electric drivetrain for performance, as described in this RS Online article. 

Concept cars: 

The GAC Hyptec SSR Baja concept is an example of an electric supercar designed to also function as a desert racer, highlighting the potential for high-end electric vehicles in this motorsport category. 

Production-based racers: 

Chevrolet created the Silverado EV ZR2, a high-performance electric race truck based on the Silverado EV, for competitions like the Mint 400. This vehicle features a powerful tri-motor setup and specialized suspension for off-road racing. 

Other examples: 

Other projects, such as the SRI EV1, have demonstrated impressive electric off-road performance with high torque, while Volkswagen has also run an electric vehicle, the ID.4, in off-road events like the Mexican 1000, as shown in this YouTube video.

The term "EV West Baja Racer" most likely refers to the SRI EV1, an electric off-road race car built by Strategic Recovery Institute in partnership with EV West, or a customer-built electric Baja Beetle, as showcased by EV West. The SRI EV1 is a high-performance buggy that competed in the Baja 1000, featuring a powerful electric powertrain designed by EV West, dual electric motors, and 535 horsepower and 750 pound-feet of torque. EV West also converts classic VW Baja Beetles into electric vehicles, often for performance and off-road use.

SRI EV1 Electric Off-Road Race Car

Partnership: 

A collaboration between the Strategic Recovery Institute (SRI) and EV West, which engineered the electric powertrain. 

Performance: 

It was built to compete in the SCORE Baja 1000 and featured dual electric motors, a sequential gearbox, and a top speed of 125 mph. 

Power: 

It produced 535 horsepower and 750 pound-feet of torque. 

Battery: 

It used a large lithium-ion battery pack for its power and had to be recharged during the race. 

EV West Custom Electric Baja Beetles

Custom builds: 

EV West also creates electric conversions of classic VW Baja Beetles, often for customers who want a high-performance off-road vehicle. 

Powertrain: 

These conversions typically use a twin-motor system and a modified stock transaxle, providing a powerful and engaging driving experience. 

DIY support: 

EV West also offers resources, such as workshops and parts like battery boxes, to help people build their own electric Baja racers.

Long periods of sitting can damage the pelvic floor by straining muscles, reducing blood flow, and increasing pressure. This can lead to issues like pelvic pain, urinary or fecal incontinence, and pelvic organ prolapse. Poor posture, such as slouching, exacerbates these problems by putting more pressure on the pelvic muscles.

How long sitting damages the pelvic floor
Muscle strain: Staying in a static position for too long can strain the pelvic floor muscles, leading to weakness or dysfunction over time. 
Poor posture: Slouching or hunching forward tilts the pelvis and increases downward pressure on the pelvic floor. 
Reduced blood flow: Prolonged sitting restricts circulation to the pelvic region, hindering the delivery of oxygen and nutrients to the muscles. 
Nerve compression: Sitting can compress nerves, like the pudendal nerve, which can lead to increased pain and dysfunction. 
Impaired function: The muscles can become weak from disuse or overstretched, weakening their ability to contract properly and support pelvic organs. 
Signs of pelvic floor damage
Pelvic or lower back pain
Urinary incontinence or urgency
Difficulty with bowel movements
Pain that runs down the legs from the lower back
Hip pain
Back or abdominal muscle spasms

Yes, an overactive or hypertonic pelvic floor can cause premature ejaculation (PE) because the muscles may not be able to relax properly, which can lower the threshold for orgasm. Weakness in these muscles can also contribute to PE, making it harder to control the timing of ejaculation.

How pelvic floor issues cause premature ejaculation
Tight/overactive pelvic floor: An overly tight pelvic floor may cause the muscles to spasm during orgasm, which can propel ejaculate out of the body too quickly. Men with this condition may not be able to relax these muscles, leading to PE. 
Weak pelvic floor: Conversely, weakness in the pelvic floor muscles can also lead to premature ejaculation because there is less muscle control to delay the ejaculation reflex. 
How to address it
Pelvic floor physical therapy: This is considered the gold standard for treating an overactive pelvic floor. A physical therapist can provide personalized exercises and techniques. 
Kegel exercises: Strengthening your pelvic floor muscles through exercises like Kegels can improve your control over ejaculation. It's important to learn how to contract and relax these muscles correctly. 
Relaxation techniques: Stretching, massage, and other relaxation techniques can help reduce tension in the pelvic floor muscles. 
Biofeedback: A therapist can use biofeedback to help you learn to properly control your pelvic floor muscles. 
Avoid overdoing it: Do not push your muscles to the extreme. If exercises cause tension or pain, stop and focus on relaxation techniques before trying strengthening exercises again.

Scientific understanding of nitric oxide and sexual function
Modern medical science has a well-established understanding of how nitric oxide (NO) affects sexual function, particularly in men. 
Role in penile erection: Nitric oxide is a key physiological signal that causes the smooth muscles in the penis to relax. This relaxation allows blood vessels to dilate, increasing blood flow into the corpus cavernosum and resulting in an erection.
Declining NO levels: Conditions such as diabetes, hypertension, and aging are associated with decreased NO activity in penile tissue, contributing to erectile dysfunction.
Supplements for NO support: Since NO itself cannot be taken as a supplement, compounds that serve as its building blocks, such as L-arginine and L-citrulline, are used to help the body produce more nitric oxide.
No link to gold ash: There is no credible scientific research indicating that gold ash (Swarna Bhasma) increases nitric oxide production in the human body. Studies on gold nanoparticles linked with NO-donating compounds are experimental and not related to the medicinal consumption of gold ash.

Pelvic floor muscle strength and sexual function
The pelvic floor muscles are essential for sexual function in both men and women.
Sexual health benefits: Pelvic floor exercises (often called Kegels) are known to strengthen these muscles, which can improve sexual function.
For men, stronger pelvic floor muscles are linked to erectile function and controlling ejaculation.
For women, these muscles contribute to sexual sensation and arousal.
Mechanism of action: These exercises work by directly conditioning the muscles that support the bladder, bowel, and reproductive organs. They operate on a biomechanical principle and have no known relationship to the ingestion of gold ash.

It’s been over 3 months since we raised this issue — and yet, Tata Motors has shown no reply and no action.
They’re experts at selling EVs, but when it comes to installing chargers, there’s zero effort. 
In Lakhisarai, Munger (Bihar) and Deoghar, Giridih, Dumka (Jharkhand), customers are still struggling for even basic charging infrastructure.
Deoghar even has an airport now, but Tata Motors still hasn’t managed to install a single DC fast charging station!
300 km no charging station.
Meanwhile, the company keeps running glossy EV ads and calling it a “green revolution.”
And when people speak the truth, their comments get deleted.
Is that what a “customer-focused” brand looks like?
If you can’t build the foundation for EV adoption, then please stop calling yourself the leader of India’s EV movement.
Right now, it feels more like marketing than progress.

Yes, you can legally drive a Right-Hand Drive (RHD) vehicle on U.S. roads, provided it meets Department of Transportation (DOT) safety standards, though some states may have specific regulations to check. Driving an RHD vehicle is challenging due to the driver's side position, making tasks like overtaking, passing, and parking potentially dangerous. However, some vehicles, like mail delivery trucks, are RHD, and the 25-year rule exempts older imported cars from modern safety standards, allowing them on the road.

To import a car into the USA, ensure it's eligible by verifying it's at least 25 years old or a Registered Importer (RI) can modify it to meet Environmental Protection Agency (EPA) and Department of Transportation (DOT) standards. Gather required documents like the bill of sale and title, complete DOT Form HS-7 and EPA Form 3520-1, and present them to U.S. Customs. You must also ensure the vehicle's undercarriage is clean of soil to meet USDA regulations.

To get a license plate in California for a newly registered vehicle, you typically receive standard sequential plates by mail 4-6 weeks after registration is complete, or you can apply for personalized or special interest plates at the DMV. For new plates, ensure your vehicle registration is up-to-date, and submit the required application and fees to the DMV.

**Major Safety and Service Concern: My Friend's Experience with the Tata Punch**

I need to share a deeply concerning safety issue that a close friend of mine has faced as an owner of the Tata Punch.

My friend realized how dangerous a child lock can be when he used it in his car. What is intended as a safety feature has unfortunately become a persistent risk to his life. He is currently struggling with a critical failure where the child lock engages, but the door cannot be opened from the inside. Despite showing this issue to multiple service executives, he has received zero solutions.

This is unfortunately not the first major issue for him:
* Approximately one year into ownership, the car's brakes failed while he was driving.
* Resolving this critical safety defect required at least four visits to the agency and took approximately three months. Even now, he sometimes experiences problems.

When my friend talks to others about the Tata Punch, they all echo the same sentiment: the service is very poor. He deeply regrets his purchase and strongly advises potential buyers to consider the significant service issues. We hope @Tata Motors and @Tata Motors Passenger Vehicles addresses these life-threatening safety and quality concerns urgently.

The petrodollar system and recycling

The global petrodollar system has two main parts:

Settlement currency: Due to a deal made between the U.S. and Saudi Arabia after the 1973 oil crisis, most international oil sales are conducted in U.S. dollars. This creates consistent global demand for the U.S. dollar.

Petrodollar recycling: Oil-exporting countries often generate more revenue than they can invest domestically. To earn a return on this surplus, they invest heavily in U.S. dollar-denominated assets, particularly U.S. Treasury securities. This process, known as petrodollar recycling, provides liquidity to global financial markets and helps finance the U.S. government's budget.

It features the BYD e4 Platform with four independent electric motors for agile performance and the DiSus-X Intelligent Body Control System for unparalleled suspension capabilities. The U9 Xtreme uses a 1200V architecture and offers a combined output of nearly 3,000 horsepower, making it the world's fastest production electric hypercar.

No, the BYD Yangwang U9 has not been launched in India; it was launched in China in February 2024, and BYD has not made official plans to bring it to the Indian market. The vehicle is BYD's pure electric supercar, priced at 1.68 million RMB (approximately ₹1.94 crore) in China, and would represent a significant entry into India's ultra-luxury segment if it were ever to be released.

I wanted to share my recent experience with my Tata Curvv EV so that others are aware before making their decision.

- During these visits service personnel ultimately advised that a sensor wiring was damaged. I doubt whether the wiring was originally damaged or tampered with while in their custody. A sensor had to be procured through insurance; delivery was delayed and incorrect parts were initially supplied. To allow urgent travel I was given a used sensor taken from another vehicle. The vehicle ran ~1,200 km and then the same drive‑mode fault recurred.

- 18 Oct 2025: Vehicle would not start; RSA jumpstart called again.

GST money doesn't go into a single dedicated bank account; instead, you pay it to the government through a Electronic Cash Ledger, which is part of the GST system, via one of the authorized banks and your bank account. You can pay through a selected bank's net banking, at the bank branch, or using other payment methods, and the funds are then transferred to your Electronic Cash Ledger.

A proper double-entry system records every financial transaction with equal debits and credits, ensuring that the books are balanced. Fraudsters exploit this system by inventing false transactions and recording corresponding debits and credits to make the books appear legitimate. The most common scheme involves fraudulent Input Tax Credit (ITC) claims using fake invoices.

Sharing My Disappointing Experience with Tata Curvv EV and After-Sales Support 

I wanted to share my recent experience with my Tata Curvv EV so that others are aware before making their decision.

I bought the Curvv EV with high expectations as it looks great and has impressive specs on paper. But my real-life experience has been extremely disappointing.

Recently, my car started showing a “Motor Temperature High” error even after being parked for nearly 48 hours. The vehicle became completely unusable. I immediately contacted the Tata RSA team, and that’s where the real struggle began.

Initially, the RSA team assured me that a towing van would arrive within one hour. After waiting and calling again, the representative on the other end suddenly started shouting at me, accusing me of abusing the towing van driver, which is completely false. I never received a single call from the towing driver in the first place.

After multiple follow-ups and wasted hours, the car was finally picked up much later than promised. It has now been at the service center for two days, and no one is responding or providing any proper update on the issue.

This entire experience has left me questioning Tata’s after-sales service, communication, and professionalism. Customers invest their trust and hard-earned money, and this kind of treatment is unacceptable, especially for a premium EV like the Curvv.

I genuinely hope Tata Motors takes feedback like this seriously and improves both their RSA response and service support system.

Has anyone else faced similar issues with Tata EVs? Would love to hear your experiences.

"Rebadge selling" refers to a practice, particularly common in the automotive industry, where an existing product is sold under a different brand name or symbol, often with minor modifications to its features, technology, or styling, to save on research and development costs while reaching different market segments or reducing product development time.

The Volkswagen Group owns a total of twelve core brands, which are divided into categories such as Core, Progressive, and Sport Luxury. These brands include the main Volkswagen and Volkswagen Commercial Vehicles, as well as Audi, Porsche, Lamborghini, Bentley, Škoda, SEAT, CUPRA, and Ducati. The group also holds commercial vehicle brands like MAN and Scania.
Why don't you sell chinese electric cars under your brand.

Many of the top electric vehicle (EV) brands are Chinese, with some dominating the global market. These companies range from large legacy manufacturers with new energy divisions to tech-focused startups. The most prominent brands include BYD, Nio, XPeng, and Li Auto.

Major electric SUV brands in China include BYD, NIO, XPeng, Li Auto, Zeekr, and Changan, which are well-known for their diverse ranges of high-performing electric vehicles, along with other brands such as Geely, SAIC Motor (AION), Great Wall Motor (Havall), and Chery.

Chinese electric sports car brands include BYD (with its Yangwang and Fangchengbao sub-brands), Xiaomi (SU7), NIO (EP9), Neta (GT), GAC (Hyper SSR), and Zeekr. These brands leverage China's EV manufacturing strength to produce high-performance electric sports cars, from accessible sporty coupes to ultra-high-performance supercars, challenging conventional automotive markets.

Major electric multi-purpose vehicle (e-MPV) brands in China include Zeekr (Zeekr 009, Zeekr Mix), Li Auto (Li Auto Mega), and BYD (Denza, Yangwang brands). Other brands like Changan's DEEPAL, GAC's Aion**, and potentially others from brands like Wuling and Great Wall Motors also offer electric vehicles that could be considered MPVs, though they may have different segment focuses.

I am writing to formally raise a complaint regarding the poor service I received at your Narol branch.
During my recent visit, I was charged **₹5,661** for my car service. I had **specifically informed your team about a wheel alignment problem** and expected it to be properly addressed. However, when I came to collect my car, I was **not allowed to inspect or test it at the service center**. I was told to check it at home and return if there were any issues — which is completely unprofessional.
After reaching home, I found that the **wheel alignment was not done properly**, and the issue remained exactly the same as before. When I went back to your workshop, I was told that a **screw was broken** and was again asked to pay **₹6,500**. This was never mentioned during the initial inspection, and it is highly unfair to ask for additional charges after I have already paid for wheel alignment and other issues such as **average and USB problems**.
Moreover, my entire day was wasted earlier as I was **called at 11:30 a.m. but received my car only at 3:00 p.m.**, which shows a lack of time management and customer care.
This experience is extremely disappointing and reflects **poor service quality and negligence**. I have already paid for the mentioned services, and it is unjustified to ask for more payment for an issue that should have been fixed during the initial service.
I request you to:
1. **Resolve all pending issues** (wheel alignment, average, and USB problems) **without any further charges**.
2. Provide a **written explanation** for why I was not informed about the broken screw earlier.
3. Ensure better service quality and communication with customers in the future.
Kindly treat this as an **urgent complaint** and take immediate action. I expect a prompt written response and proper service of my car without charging again within 7 working days otherwise I will escalate the matter to Consume district court as well as in Social media.
**Service Details:**
* Car Model: TATA PUNCH
* Registration Number: GJ27EE6239
* Date of Service: 22/09/2025
* Invoice Number: IYMGAH2526007163

**Major Safety and Service Concern: My Friend's Experience with the Tata Punch**

I need to share a deeply concerning safety issue that a close friend of mine has faced as an owner of the Tata Punch.

My friend realized how dangerous a child lock can be when he used it in his car. What is intended as a safety feature has unfortunately become a persistent risk to his life. He is currently struggling with a critical failure where the child lock engages, but the door cannot be opened from the inside. Despite showing this issue to multiple service executives, he has received zero solutions.

This is unfortunately not the first major issue for him:
* Approximately one year into ownership, the car's brakes failed while he was driving.
* Resolving this critical safety defect required at least four visits to the agency and took approximately three months. Even now, he sometimes experiences problems.

When my friend talks to others about the Tata Punch, they all echo the same sentiment: the service is very poor. He deeply regrets his purchase and strongly advises potential buyers to consider the significant service issues. Subject: Urgent Complaint Regarding Poor Vehicle Quality & Service – Tata Nexon (GJ32AG2244)
To,
The Customer Care / Head Office,
Tata Motors Limited
Respected Sir/Madam,
My name is Ashok Nakum, and I purchased a Tata Nexon (Vehicle No. GJ32AG2244) from Bhagvati Tata Motors, Veraval. Unfortunately, my experience with both the vehicle and the service has been extremely disappointing, and I am compelled to escalate this matter to you.
From the time of delivery itself, the vehicle had multiple issues which were neither checked nor informed to me:
1. Reverse sensor (right side) not working at the time of delivery.
2. Right side headlight was defective.
3. Main engine wiring damaged, which is still not replaced despite repeated complaints.
4. Driving modes (Sports, Eco, City) are not functioning at all.
5. On long journeys, the engine suddenly shuts down and restarts on its own, creating major safety concerns.
6. The display also stops working intermittently.
I had trusted Tata Motors for its quality and reliability, which is why I chose the Nexon. However, instead of enjoying my new car, I have faced constant problems and have had to visit the service center around 10 times already, wasting my time and peace of mind.
What makes the situation worse is the poor customer handling by Bhagvati Tata Motors, Veraval. When I raised these concerns with their staff, instead of accepting responsibility, they behaved unprofessionally. In fact, during one of my conversations with a lady staff member from the sales department, my call was abruptly cut, which I found highly disrespectful.
This is not the level of service and product quality I expected from Tata Motors. Such negligence not only causes harassment but also raises serious safety risks for me and my family.
I request Tata Motors Head Office to:
Immediately resolve all the above-mentioned issues with my car at the earliest.
Take strict action against the dealer for such poor service and unprofessional behavior.
Compensate me for the repeated inconvenience, time loss, and mental stress caused.
I hope Tata Motors values its customers and addresses this complaint with seriousness and urgency.

Title: Heartbroken and Betrayed by Tata Motors – Worst Experience with Tigor
As a 60+ year old hardworking man who lived a life without deceit or shortcuts, buying a car was never just a status symbol for me — it was a dream built through decades of sweat and sacrifice.
After retiring from work, and with improved family stability (thanks to my wife’s job and daughter’s UK placement after her Tata scholarship), we decided to buy a brand-new Tata Tigor. It was my daughter’s strong wish, out of respect for Tata’s values. I too believed in Tata’s ideals — nation-building and ethics.
We exchanged our trusted Maruti Eeco, paid ₹7 lakhs and brought home the Tigor with pride.
But just a month later, during monsoon, I discovered:
Water stagnating inside the cabin
Unusual noises from within
Gear shifting issues
We trusted Tata’s brand and assumed the first service would fix it all. But that was only the beginning of a year-long nightmare. Repeated complaints. Garage visits. False assurances. Zero accountability. Even after a legal notice, nothing changed. The lawyer I hired backed out — possibly due to pressure or threats.
After finally deciding to return the defective car, Tata’s team valued my barely-used Tigor (insured at ₹7+ lakhs) at just ₹4.5 lakhs. All problems were from their side, yet they refused to take responsibility.
Out of frustration and heartbreak, I even considered burning the car rather than handing it over to another innocent buyer. I didn’t, only because I feared it might be misused if it reached the wrong hands.
This experience has shattered my trust in Tata Motors. A company we once saw as a pillar of Indian values now treats common people with complete neglect.
To the management at Tata Motors:  Is this how you treat loyal Indian customers who trusted you over foreign brands?  Where is your quality control?  Why must the poor fight for basic accountability while your brand profits?
Please take this as a wake-up call — fix your processes, own your mistakes, and stop hiding behind your dealerships. Tata should mean trust — not trauma.
Vehicle No: MH48CT 8250
Model: Tata Tigor XZ+ CNG
Location: Vasai, Maharashtra
Purchased: 2024
