Sharing My Disappointing Experience with Tata Curvv EV and After-Sales Support 

I wanted to share my recent experience with my Tata Curvv EV so that others are aware before making their decision.

I bought the Curvv EV with high expectations as it looks great and has impressive specs on paper. But my real-life experience has been extremely disappointing.

Recently, my car started showing a “Motor Temperature High” error even after being parked for nearly 48 hours. The vehicle became completely unusable. I immediately contacted the Tata RSA team, and that’s where the real struggle began.

Initially, the RSA team assured me that a towing van would arrive within one hour. After waiting and calling again, the representative on the other end suddenly started shouting at me, accusing me of abusing the towing van driver, which is completely false. I never received a single call from the towing driver in the first place.

After multiple follow-ups and wasted hours, the car was finally picked up much later than promised. It has now been at the service center for two days, and no one is responding or providing any proper update on the issue.

This entire experience has left me questioning Tata’s after-sales service, communication, and professionalism. Customers invest their trust and hard-earned money, and this kind of treatment is unacceptable, especially for a premium EV like the Curvv.

I genuinely hope Tata Motors takes feedback like this seriously and improves both their RSA response and service support system.

Has anyone else faced similar issues with Tata EVs? Would love to hear your experiences.

#TataCurvvEV #TataMotors #EVIndia #CustomerExperience #TataEV

**Major Safety and Service Concern: My Friend's Experience with the Tata Punch**

I need to share a deeply concerning safety issue that a close friend of mine has faced as an owner of the Tata Punch.

My friend realized how dangerous a child lock can be when he used it in his car. What is intended as a safety feature has unfortunately become a persistent risk to his life. He is currently struggling with a critical failure where the child lock engages, but the door cannot be opened from the inside. Despite showing this issue to multiple service executives, he has received zero solutions.

This is unfortunately not the first major issue for him:
* Approximately one year into ownership, the car's brakes failed while he was driving.
* Resolving this critical safety defect required at least four visits to the agency and took approximately three months. Even now, he sometimes experiences problems.

When my friend talks to others about the Tata Punch, they all echo the same sentiment: the service is very poor. He deeply regrets his purchase and strongly advises potential buyers to consider the significant service issues. As a 60+ year old hardworking man who lived a life without deceit or shortcuts, buying a car was never just a status symbol for me — it was a dream built through decades of sweat and sacrifice.
After retiring from work, and with improved family stability (thanks to my wife’s job and daughter’s UK placement after her Tata scholarship), we decided to buy a brand-new Tata Tigor. It was my daughter’s strong wish, out of respect for Tata’s values. I too believed in Tata’s ideals — nation-building and ethics.
We exchanged our trusted Maruti Eeco, paid ₹7 lakhs and brought home the Tigor with pride.
But just a month later, during monsoon, I discovered:
Water stagnating inside the cabin
Unusual noises from within
Gear shifting issues
We trusted Tata’s brand and assumed the first service would fix it all. But that was only the beginning of a year-long nightmare. Repeated complaints. Garage visits. False assurances. Zero accountability. Even after a legal notice, nothing changed. The lawyer I hired backed out — possibly due to pressure or threats.
After finally deciding to return the defective car, Tata’s team valued my barely-used Tigor (insured at ₹7+ lakhs) at just ₹4.5 lakhs. All problems were from their side, yet they refused to take responsibility.
Out of frustration and heartbreak, I even considered burning the car rather than handing it over to another innocent buyer. I didn’t, only because I feared it might be misused if it reached the wrong hands.
This experience has shattered my trust in Tata Motors. A company we once saw as a pillar of Indian values now treats common people with complete neglect.
To the management at Tata Motors:  Is this how you treat loyal Indian customers who trusted you over foreign brands?  Where is your quality control?  Why must the poor fight for basic accountability while your brand profits?
Please take this as a wake-up call — fix your processes, own your mistakes, and stop hiding behind your dealerships. Tata should mean trust — not trauma.
Vehicle No: MH48CT 8250
Model: Tata Tigor XZ+ CNG
Location: Vasai, Maharashtra
Purchased: 2024

	•	I visited the service centre twice, but they only did calibration/alignment without finding the real problem.
	•	Later, at a local tyre shop, I was told the lower arm was bent – something the Tata service team completely failed to diagnose.
	•	I submitted my car again, and they promised delivery in 2 days, but kept it for 4 days without working on it properly.
	•	Most of the time, the car was simply parked without being checked.
	•	Even after replacing the lower arm, the issue was still not resolved.
	•	CRM and Service Advisor never picked up calls, and the manager behaved very rudely, even saying: “If you don’t have time, take your car and go to another service centre.”

As a professional photographer, I depend on my car daily. Their negligence wasted my valuable working days and caused unnecessary stress.

Yes, Tata Motors can show ads to U.S. customers, primarily through its owned brands, Jaguar and Land Rover, which are popular luxury car brands in America. Although Tata Motors does not sell its own branded passenger cars in the U.S., its luxury subsidiary, JLR, is a significant market, and Tata Motors engages in advertising and marketing activities for these brands within the U.S..

Major US truck manufacturers include Daimler Truck (owning Freightliner and Western Star), Paccar (owning Kenworth and Peterbilt), Navistar (maker of International trucks), and Mack Trucks. Volvo Trucks, a separate but related company to Mack, also operates in the US market. Other truck and commercial vehicle manufacturers with a presence in the US include Ford, Hino, Isuzu, and Mitsubishi Fuso.

Tata trucks can compete with Peterbilt trucks in their respective primary markets, but direct competition is limited due to differing design philosophies, target applications, and engine power. Peterbilt trucks are built for high-horsepower, long-haul American roads, emphasizing durability, power, and advanced features. Tata trucks are known for their reliability, low maintenance costs, and affordability, specifically designed to meet the needs of Indian roads and various commercial applications.

Tata Motors has been consistently reducing its total debt, which stood at approximately ₹770.7 billion for the quarter ending March 2025, down from about ₹1.12 trillion a year prior. The company also holds significant cash reserves, resulting in a net debt of about ₹96.4 billion. This reduction in debt is a part of a strategic turnaround, including a debt-free status for its Indian commercial and passenger vehicle business.

In India, legal tender for debt repayment includes all banknotes issued by the Reserve Bank of India (RBI) and all coins issued by the Government of India that are not withdrawn from circulation and have not been defaced or lost excessive weight. These are legally recognized forms of payment that must be accepted for public and private debts. However, the legal tender status of notes can be changed by the government, as seen with the demonetization of the old ₹500 and ₹1000 notes in 2016.

Title: Heartbroken and Betrayed by Tata Motors – Worst Experience with Tigor
As a 60+ year old hardworking man who lived a life without deceit or shortcuts, buying a car was never just a status symbol for me — it was a dream built through decades of sweat and sacrifice.
After retiring from work, and with improved family stability (thanks to my wife’s job and daughter’s UK placement after her Tata scholarship), we decided to buy a brand-new Tata Tigor. It was my daughter’s strong wish, out of respect for Tata’s values. I too believed in Tata’s ideals — nation-building and ethics.
We exchanged our trusted Maruti Eeco, paid ₹7 lakhs and brought home the Tigor with pride.
But just a month later, during monsoon, I discovered:
Water stagnating inside the cabin
Unusual noises from within
Gear shifting issues
We trusted Tata’s brand and assumed the first service would fix it all. But that was only the beginning of a year-long nightmare. Repeated complaints. Garage visits. False assurances. Zero accountability. Even after a legal notice, nothing changed. The lawyer I hired backed out — possibly due to pressure or threats.
After finally deciding to return the defective car, Tata’s team valued my barely-used Tigor (insured at ₹7+ lakhs) at just ₹4.5 lakhs. All problems were from their side, yet they refused to take responsibility.
Out of frustration and heartbreak, I even considered burning the car rather than handing it over to another innocent buyer. I didn’t, only because I feared it might be misused if it reached the wrong hands.
This experience has shattered my trust in Tata Motors. A company we once saw as a pillar of Indian values now treats common people with complete neglect.
To the management at Tata Motors:  Is this how you treat loyal Indian customers who trusted you over foreign brands?  Where is your quality control?  Why must the poor fight for basic accountability while your brand profits?
Please take this as a wake-up call — fix your processes, own your mistakes, and stop hiding behind your dealerships. Tata should mean trust — not trauma.
Vehicle No: MH48CT 8250
Model: Tata Tigor XZ+ CNG
Location: Vasai, Maharashtra
Purchased: 2024

Double-entry bookkeeping, also known as double-entry accounting, is a method of bookkeeping that relies on a two-sided accounting entry to maintain financial information. Every entry into an account requires a corresponding and opposite entry into a different account. The double-entry system has two equal and corresponding sides, known as debit and credit; this is based on the fundamental accounting principle that for every debit, there must be an equal and opposite credit.

My Experience with Tata Motors Varenyam JK Road Bhopal Service Center 

करीब एक साल पहले मेरी कार का accident हुआ था। Repair के लिए मैंने अपनी कार Tata Motors Varenyam JK Road, Bhopal service center पर दी।

Service के बाद भी मेरी कार का rear axle damage ठीक नहीं हुआ। उस समय मुझे इसकी जानकारी नहीं थी और मैंने ध्यान भी नहीं दिया। कुछ समय बाद जब मुझे इस issue का पता चला, तो मैंने दोबारा service center में जाकर इसकी शिकायत दर्ज की।

शिकायत के दौरान मुझे यह बताया गया कि “यह damage पहले से था और हमारी तरफ से कोई गलती नहीं हुई।”
मेरे लिए यह सुनना बहुत disappointing था, क्योंकि कार repair के लिए ही दी गई थी।

बाद में जब मैंने Tata Varenyam के Instagram page पर अपनी बात comments में लिखनी चाही, तो मेरा account block कर दिया गया।

@tatamotorscars I am from a lower middle-class background, and I have a deep liking for Tata. Tata feels like “ours,” and whenever I think about buying a car in the future, my first thought is always Tata. In fact, I feel strongly that I should not buy anything other than a Tata car.

However, when I look at the designs of your passenger cars, I often feel confused whether to admire them or to laugh at them. Compared to Hyundai and Suzuki, Tata cars have fewer options, and even among them, the designs don’t feel modern enough or updated to match the current generation’s expectations.

For example, if I compare Hyundai Creta, Maruti Suzuki Brezza, and Hyundai Venue with Tata’s lineup, the only comparable car is Nexon. If someone doesn’t like the Nexon, what other options do you give them? Tata Punch and Nexon both have a rough and tough look, but where is a car with a classy, professional appearance? Tiago and Altroz, though nice, look too small—almost like matchboxes. I don’t understand what your design team is doing.

In today’s highly competitive market, people expect multiple options. But Tata seems to behave as though customers must choose only from the limited four cars available. The mentality of India’s middle class is changing. They don’t just want small cars like Tiago or Altroz; they want at least mid-sized vehicles like Venue, Creta, or Brezza.

I sincerely suggest you conduct more research, talk to people, and understand what type of designs and cars they prefer, then update your models accordingly. Otherwise, many like me—who are loyal to Tata—may be forced to switch to Hyundai or Suzuki.

Still, I thank Tata Motors and your wonderful team for your dedication. I truly wish to see Tata leading with stylish, modern, and competitive cars in the passenger segment.

Double-entry bookkeeping, also known as double-entry accounting, is a method of bookkeeping that relies on a two-sided accounting entry to maintain financial information. Every entry into an account requires a corresponding and opposite entry into a different account. The double-entry system has two equal and corresponding sides, known as debit and credit; this is based on the fundamental accounting principle that for every debit, there must be an equal and opposite credit. A transaction in double-entry bookkeeping always affects at least two accounts, always includes at least one debit and one credit, and always has total debits and total credits that are equal. The purpose of double-entry bookkeeping is to allow the detection of financial errors and fraud.

	1.	Auto gear box lag & gear stuck issues

	4.	Parking sensors beep continuously without objects
	5.	TPMS fault – never works
	6.	Wireless charger heats phone & doesn’t charge or charge very slow
	7.	Auto hold mode gives a heavy jerk while accelerating
	8.	Incorrect fuel efficiency readings (tank full shows 350–400km only, though FE is 15–16 kmpl)
	9.	Diesel filter not available even after 1 month (car at 19k km)
	10.	DRL gaps (possible manufacturing defect, told “normal”)
	11.	Tail light hangs while locking/unlocking
	12.	Music system resets to factory settings automatically
	13.	Welcome seats work randomly
	14.	Harrier feels unusually heavy to drive (technician says “top model is heavier”)
15. paint is chipping off in 6 months 

These are only some of the many issues I’ve faced. A few got resolved, but most remain pending.

I need to share a deeply concerning safety issue that a close friend of mine has faced as an owner of the Tata Punch.

My friend realized how dangerous a child lock can be when he used it in his car. What is intended as a safety feature has unfortunately become a persistent risk to his life. He is currently struggling with a critical failure where the child lock engages, but the door cannot be opened from the inside. Despite showing this issue to multiple service executives, he has received zero solutions.

This is unfortunately not the first major issue for him:
* Approximately one year into ownership, the car's brakes failed while he was driving.

When my friend talks to others about the Tata Punch, they all echo the same sentiment: the service is very poor. @tatamotors @tatamotors.ev @tatamotorspassengervehicles – Customer safety must be a priority!

#TataMotors #TiagoEV #EVIndia #ElectricVehicle #CustomerExperience #RoadsideAssistance #CustomerSafety #Unacceptable #EVProblems

Great ad by the way , but about build quality and service of your cars ; My three months of Tata Punch EV experience have been nothing short of a nightmare. The charging sessions would intermittently stop, and each time I had to restart them by either opening the car door or refreshing the app. When I first reported this issue, they checked and said it was just a software glitch that had been corrected, assuring me it wouldn’t happen again. But the issue persisted the very next day when I put it on charge. Once again, I took the car to the service center, and they said there was no problem with charging, claiming it didn’t stop. However, when I checked the app, I noticed that charging had stopped seven times during that single session. I wonder how the people at the service center failed to notice that.
They asked me to share my app data—information they should already have had. Only after that did they agree there was a problem and start checking it. It’s been 10 days now, and every day when I call for updates, they just say they’ve made some changes. It feels like they are working purely on a trial-and-error basis. They have absolutely no clue what’s happening. It seems they are playing guessing games and using customers’ cars for training. Honestly, I don’t even think the people at the service center are qualified.
Just producing cars and advertising them is not enough for a company like Tata; it must ensure smooth after-sales service as well. If you can’t provide effective after-sales service, you shouldn’t position yourself as a leading company in EVs. If your own service team cannot understand or resolve the issue, how are we supposed to trust your technology? It raises a fundamental question: if you don’t know how to repair your car, why did you build it in the first place?

Tata Motors – Is This What Customers Deserve?

We spent ₹23 lakhs on a brand-new Tata Curvv EV, expecting safety, reliability, and a premium experience. Instead, what we got is a complete nightmare.

In just 6 months of ownership, our car has spent 4 months at the service center. Every other week, there’s a new failure:
	•	System crashes and software failures
	•	App malfunctioning
	•	AC not working
	•	Wipers not functioning
	•	Charging glitches
	•	Sudden power cuts while driving
	•	Touchscreen freezing and blackouts
	•	Random breakdowns leaving us stranded

This isn’t just harassment — it’s a serious risk to our lives. If tomorrow we face a medical emergency or an urgent situation and this car breaks down again, will Tata Motors take responsibility?

We paid a fortune, but what we’ve received is an unreliable, unsafe car that has robbed us of peace of mind. Customers deserve better — not endless trips to service centers and empty assurances.

@tatamotorscars — It’s time you answer. Will you replace this defective car, or will you continue to ignore your customers’ safety? @tatacurvv @tatamotorscars @tatamotorsgroup

Stop selling Cars till you fix After sale services! 

Tata is waste of Money 27lacs wasted ! 

Extremely disappointed with Tata Motors service at Zedex Tata Wazirpur.
Since day one of purchasing my Tata Harrier, I have been facing constant issues, and even after multiple visits and sharing photos/videos, no resolution has been provided.

Issues I am still facing:
	1.	Auto gear box lag & gear stuck issues
	2.	AC knob not working since Day 1
	3.	Brake pad whistle & clicking sound, heavy carbon deposit
	4.	Parking sensors beep continuously without objects
	5.	TPMS fault – never works
	6.	Wireless charger heats phone & doesn’t charge or charge very slow
	7.	Auto hold mode gives a heavy jerk while accelerating
	8.	Incorrect fuel efficiency readings (tank full shows 350–400km only, though FE is 15–16 kmpl)
	9.	Diesel filter not available even after 1 month (car at 19k km)
	10.	DRL gaps (possible manufacturing defect, told “normal”)
	11.	Tail light hangs while locking/unlocking
	12.	Music system resets to factory settings automatically
	13.	Welcome seats work randomly
	14.	Harrier feels unusually heavy to drive (technician says “top model is heavier”)
15. paint is chipping off in 6 months 

These are only some of the many issues I’ve faced. A few got resolved, but most remain pending.

 Main problem is not the car but the poor service, lack of trained staff, and zero accountability. Every time I visit the service center, staff has changed and nothing gets fixed properly.

Tata really needs to look into this. Customers don’t expect such service after investing in a premium vehicle.

#tataharrier #TataMotors #tata

Global demand for electric vehicles (EVs) is experiencing significant growth, with sales exceeding 17 million in 2024 and accounting for over 20% of new car sales. Projections indicate continued robust growth, with sales potentially reaching 30 million by 2027, according to EVBoosters. This growth is driven by factors such as government incentives, improvements in charging infrastructure, and increased affordability of EVs.

Top countries for electric vehicle (EV) adoption

Norway stands out with the highest EV adoption rates globally. In 2022, 80% of new passenger vehicle sales were all-electric. In fact, in 2024, Norway became the first country where the number of electric cars surpassed the number of petrol cars registered. This rapid shift is driven by generous government incentives, such as tax breaks, toll exemptions, reduced parking fees for EV owners, and a dense public fast-charging network.

China, despite being the largest car market in the world, holds a significant share of EV sales (22% in 2022). The country has seen exponential growth in EV sales, with nearly half of its car sales being electric in 2024, and accounting for almost two-thirds of global EV sales. This is attributed to government support, including a trade-in scheme and other incentives, and a focus on developing affordable EV models.

Other leading European countries include Iceland (41% EV sales share in 2022), Sweden (32%), and the Netherlands (24%). These countries, similar to Norway, have implemented supportive policies and developed charging infrastructure to facilitate the transition.

The United States shows rapid growth in EV sales, although its market share (9.5% in 2023) still lags behind European and Chinese leaders. The U.S. is investing heavily in charging infrastructure and offers tax credits to encourage EV adoption.

Tata Motors – Is This What Customers Deserve?

We spent ₹23 lakhs on a brand-new Tata Curvv EV, expecting safety, reliability, and a premium experience. Instead, what we got is a complete nightmare.

In just 6 months of ownership, our car has spent 4 months at the service center. Every other week, there’s a new failure:
	•	System crashes and software failures
	•	App malfunctioning
	•	AC not working
	•	Wipers not functioning
	•	Charging glitches
	•	Sudden power cuts while driving
	•	Touchscreen freezing and blackouts
	•	Random breakdowns leaving us stranded

This isn’t just harassment — it’s a serious risk to our lives. If tomorrow we face a medical emergency or an urgent situation and this car breaks down again, will Tata Motors take responsibility?

We paid a fortune, but what we’ve received is an unreliable, unsafe car that has robbed us of peace of mind. Customers deserve better — not endless trips to service centers and empty assurances.

Dear Tata Motors Customer Care,

I am compelled to write this email after months of disappointment, frustration, and repeated failures by Tata Motors and its authorized dealer to resolve the serious issues with my Tata Punch, purchased in July 2023.

This car has been nothing but a liability since the day of purchase. Instead of being on the road, it has spent most of its time in the garage, with recurring breakdowns and unresolved mechanical failures.

At this point, I have completely lost faith in this vehicle and in the so-called “repairs” being carried out. This is not just an inconvenience — it is a safety hazard and a violation of consumer rights.

Therefore, I demand the following:
	•	Immediate refund of the full value of the car OR a replacement with a new defect-free vehicle.
	•	Full accountability for the money, time, and mental stress caused by Tata’s failure to deliver a reliable product.
	•	A written confirmation of the steps Tata Motors will take to resolve this matter within 7 working days.

If this issue is not resolved immediately, I will have no choice but to escalate this to consumer court, regulatory authorities, social media, and automotive forums to ensure no other customer suffers the way we have.

I expect an urgent response with a clear action plan. I trusted Tata Motors because of its legacy, but this experience has completely shaken that trust. It is now up to Tata to show whether it stands behind its customers and its products.

Tata Motors – Is This What Customers Deserve?

We spent ₹23 lakhs on a brand-new Tata Curvv EV, expecting safety, reliability, and a premium experience. Instead, what we got is a complete nightmare.

In just 6 months of ownership, our car has spent 4 months at the service center. Every other week, there’s a new failure:
	•	System crashes and software failures
	•	App malfunctioning
	•	AC not working
	•	Wipers not functioning
	•	Charging glitches
	•	Sudden power cuts while driving
	•	Touchscreen freezing and blackouts
	•	Random breakdowns leaving us stranded

This isn’t just harassment — it’s a serious risk to our lives. If tomorrow we face a medical emergency or an urgent situation and this car breaks down again, will Tata Motors take responsibility?

We paid a fortune, but what we’ve received is an unreliable, unsafe car that has robbed us of peace of mind. Customers deserve better — not endless trips to service centers and empty assurances.

@tatamotorscars — It’s time you answer. Will you replace this defective car, or will you continue to ignore your customers’ safety? @tatacurvv @tatamotorscars @tatamotorsgroup

Dear Sir/Madam,

This email is a formal escalation against the completely unacceptable behaviour and false commitments made by your service staff at Berkeley tata chandigarh and Jitendra Mahura regarding the AC compressor replacement of my Tata Hexa.

My AC compressor, which was replaced about an year ago, has failed again. Your team initially assured me multiple times that the issue would be addressed and supported, given the repeated failure. But after keeping me waiting for hours , I was suddenly informed that the commitment “cannot be honoured.”

This is nothing short of misleading, unprofessional, and negligent.

I am shocked that Tata Motors—a brand that claims customer-first service—allows its staff to give false verbal assurances and then conveniently back out. This is not a minor miscommunication; it is a breach of trust and a deliberate attempt to misguide a customer.

"मैं धारक को 500 रुपये अदा करने का वचन देता हूँ" का मतलब है कि भारतीय रिजर्व बैंक (RBI) धारक को 500 रुपये के बराबर मूल्य की वस्तु या सेवा देने का वचन देता है। यह एक कानूनी आश्वासन है कि 500 रुपये का नोट या सिक्का 500 रुपये के बराबर मूल्य का है। 

यह वाक्य भारतीय करेंसी नोटों पर लिखा होता है और इसका मतलब है कि RBI उस नोट के मूल्य का भुगतान करने की जिम्मेदारी लेता है, according to Vikaspedia. इसका मतलब है कि यदि आपके पास 500 रुपये का नोट है, तो RBI आपको 500 रुपये के बराबर मूल्य की वस्तु या सेवा देने के लिए बाध्य है, says News18.

I’ve always defended @tatamotorscars , proudly owned one for years without a single issue. Solid cars, great build, zero complaints. So naturally, when it was time to upgrade, Tata was my first choice.

And then came the Harrier EV test drive. Honestly, it felt less like a test drive and more like a punishment. From the moment we entered, the dealership experience was cold and unwelcoming. The best part? If you’re late by a few minutes, their solution is simple — no test drive for you. Imagine spending 30+ lakh and being treated like you’re asking for a favor instead of planning to buy their car.

Have you as a company ever filed an police FIR for bad roads in India ?

While filing an FIR (First Information Report) for bad roads in India is not a typical scenario, it can be done in specific cases where the poor road conditions directly lead to an accident or injury. Generally, complaints about bad roads are directed towards civic authorities or municipal corporations, who are responsible for road maintenance.

Speed bumps in India are often poorly designed, leading to discomfort, vehicle damage, and even accidents. Unscientific placement, inadequate construction, and a lack of proper markings are common issues. This results in negative impacts on traffic flow, vehicle maintenance, and the safety of vulnerable road users.

Yes, speed bumps can potentially cause spinal cord damage, particularly in situations involving improper installation, excessive speed, or vulnerable individuals. While not a common occurrence, spinal fractures, especially at the thoracolumbar junction, have been documented in cases involving vehicles passing over speed bumps.

Dear Tata Motors Team,
I hope this message finds you well.
I am writing to raise a serious concern regarding multiple pending deliverables related to my Tata Safari (Vehicle No: UP80HQ9888) purchased from Ashok Tata, Cosmos Mall, Agra, approximately 1.5 months ago. I made the entire payment in cash, including an advance amount, but several promises made by the dealership remain unfulfilled. Despite regular follow-ups over the past two months, I have received no proper response or resolution.
Here are the key issues:
1. Advance Refund: A portion of the advance amount is still pending and has not been returned to me.
2. FASTag & Screen Guards: I was promised a complimentary FASTag and 4 screen guards by the showroom team.
However, even after 1.5 months of waiting, I did not receive them. Meanwhile, scratches had already started to appear on the vehicle, so I was left with no option but to get both the FASTag and screen guards installed from outside.
Therefore, I request a refund for the cost of FASTag and 4 screen guards, since they were never provided by the showroom.
3. Extended Warranty: I had paid for the extended warranty at the time of purchase, but I have not yet received any confirmation or documentation.

I had trusted Tata Motors for a smooth and reliable vehicle ownership experience, but the after-sales support so far has been quite frustrating. I request your immediate attention and resolution of the above points, along with the refund for the FASTag and screen guards.

The lack of support from the salesperson and the service center's initial response has been frustrating. I'm concerned about the reliability of Goodyear tyres and the overall ownership experience of Tata Curvv.
*Cons:*
- Poor tyre quality (Goodyear)
- Unresponsive salesperson
- Unsatisfactory service center experience
- High cost of replacement tyre
*Recommendation:*

Only 3 years of warranty is active. No extended warranty (2 years) has been added.
Free services before 13,000 km have lapsed, and I will only be eligible for services due post that.
No extra service support will be provided as promised.

3. National Consumer Disputes Redressal Commission (NCDRC) has held in various judgments (e.g., Maruti Udyog Ltd. v. Susheel Kumar Gabgotra, 2006) that misrepresentation in vehicle warranty/services amounts to unfair trade practice.

Background & Assurances Given:
At the time of enquiry and personal meetings prior to purchase, your sales representatives had clearly assured me of the following:

Free services before 13,000 km have lapsed, and I will only be eligible for services due post that.
No extra service support will be provided as promised.
This is a clear case of mis-selling, misrepresentation and breach of pre-sale commitments, amounting to deficiency in service under Indian consumer protection law.
Legal Grounds:
1. Section 18, Indian Contract Act, 1872 – Misrepresentation.
2. Section 2(1)(r) of Consumer Protection Act, 2019 – Defines unfair trade practices including false promises and misleading assurances.
3. National Consumer Disputes Redressal Commission (NCDRC) has held in various judgments (e.g., Maruti Udyog Ltd. v. Susheel Kumar Gabgotra, 2006) that misrepresentation in vehicle warranty/services amounts to unfair trade practice.

Title: Extremely Disappointed with Tata Curvv – Clutch Failed Twice Within a Month!

I bought a Tata Curvv just 6 months ago, expecting quality and reliability from a trusted Indian brand. Unfortunately, my experience has been nothing short of a nightmare. The clutch failed not once, but TWICE within a gap of just 15 days!

When I approached the Tata service center, instead of investigating the issue properly, they conveniently blamed my driving skills — despite the fact that my driving rating is 4.9 and I’ve been driving a Maruti car for the past 13 years without a single clutch issue. Clearly, the problem lies with the quality of parts or manufacturing, not the driver.

To make matters worse, the service center seems more interested in piling up high repair bills rather than solving the root cause. It feels like they’re trying to make money off a defect instead of taking responsibility for a clearly faulty product.

Tata Motors seriously needs to rethink their quality control and customer service. I never expected such poor reliability and after-sales support. If this is the experience within the first 6 months, I fear what’s ahead.

I’ll be sharing this review across Google, Facebook, Twitter, Instagram, and any platform I can, so that others don’t make the same mistake. Think twice before investing in the Tata Curvv.

First Fault (Feb 2025): Within just 3 months, the front left shock absorber failed. The Bokaro service centre initially refused to acknowledge the issue. Only after multiple visits and a live demonstration did they accept it, and it took 10–12 days to replace it.

Clutch Issue Ignored: At the same time, I reported a clutch plate issue, which they dismissed without proper inspection.

Breakdown (June 2025): The clutch problem resurfaced in June, causing a complete breakdown. My vehicle was towed to the Dhanbad Barwada service centre, where your team failed to detect the clutch issue and instead billed me ₹2,000 for a so-called battery circuit problem.

Repeat Failure: Just 10 days later, the car stopped again. The engine refused to start for 20–25 minutes, and an engine check light appeared, only to disappear after 2 hours.

Latest Breakdown: Recently, after reporting the clutch issue again, the car broke down within 4 hours. It is now once again towed to the Barwada service centre.

3. Share an official update or action plan from the technical team.

**Major Safety and Service Concern: My Friend's Experience with the Tata Punch**

I need to share a deeply concerning safety issue that a close friend of mine has faced as an owner of the Tata Punch.

My friend realized how dangerous a child lock can be when he used it in his car. What is intended as a safety feature has unfortunately become a persistent risk to his life. He is currently struggling with a critical failure where the child lock engages, but the door cannot be opened from the inside. Despite showing this issue to multiple service executives, he has received zero solutions.

* Approximately one year into ownership, the car's brakes failed while he was driving.
* Resolving this critical safety defect required at least four visits to the agency and took approximately three months. Even now, he sometimes experiences problems.

When my friend talks to others about the Tata Punch, they all echo the same sentiment: the service is very poor. He deeply regrets his purchase and strongly advises potential buyers to consider the significant service issues. We hope @Tata Motors and @Tata Motors Passenger Vehicles addresses these life-threatening safety and quality concerns urgently.

I, Mrs. Santosh devi Khandelwal, representing Santosh Sales Corporation, am writing this formal complaint against your dealership Brijwheel Automobiles Pvt. Ltd., Bharatpur, for mis-selling and breach of verbal and written assurances at the time of selling a demo vehicle (Tata Safari – Chassis No: MAT631702RWA02386, Engine No: 46335451-4288018), delivered to me on 08.06.2025.
Background & Assurances Given:
At the time of enquiry and personal meetings prior to purchase, your sales representatives had clearly assured me of the following:
1. 5-year warranty (3-year standard + 2-year extended warranty) considering it was a demo car.
2. Free services as applicable to a new car, regardless of demo usage.
3. That the demo usage (approximately 13,000 km) would not affect my post-sale service schedule or benefits.
These assurances were key deciding factors for purchasing the demo vehicle and were reiterated multiple times both verbally and on call before the transaction.
The Issue:
After delivery, the vehicle started malfunctioning and I took it to Tech Wheel Service Centre, Sitapura, Jaipur, where I was informed:
Only 3 years of warranty is active. No extended warranty (2 years) has been added.
Free services before 13,000 km have lapsed, and I will only be eligible for services due post that.
No extra service support will be provided as promised.
This is a clear case of mis-selling, misrepresentation and breach of pre-sale commitments, amounting to deficiency in service under Indian consumer protection law.
Legal Grounds:
1. Section 18, Indian Contract Act, 1872 – Misrepresentation.
2. Section 2(1)(r) of Consumer Protection Act, 2019 – Defines unfair trade practices including false promises and misleading assurances.
3. National Consumer Disputes Redressal Commission (NCDRC) has held in various judgments (e.g., Maruti Udyog Ltd. v. Susheel Kumar Gabgotra, 2006) that misrepresentation in vehicle warranty/services amounts to unfair trade practice.
4. Section 17, Indian Contract Act, 1872 – Fraud

I recently purchased the Tata Curvv EV and had a very disappointing experience. During the test drive, I asked several questions about the EV features, but the consultant had no idea about EV technology. Even the sales consultant lacked basic knowledge about charging and usage. I had to learn everything from other EV users.

The major issue was the misleading information they gave me about free charging. I was promised free access to Tata Power charging stations for 6 months after purchase. But after delivery, they told me this offer was only for older Curvv models from 2024 and that they had no clear knowledge about it.

I raised complaints with higher authorities, but nothing was resolved. This shows how poorly informed and irresponsible Tata’s customer service is. I strongly advise others to think twice before going for Tata EVs — the post-sales support and customer care are extremely disappointing.

I am very interested in purchasing the Tata Curvv and am particularly keen on owning it in a Matte Black finish. I have heard about the possibility of a “Stealth Edition” or similar special editions and wanted to ask if there are any official plans to launch such variants.

I am willing to pay extra for a company-backed customization if that is an option. My preference is to have a special edition or customized variant that carries the official touch and quality assurance of Tata Motors.

सड़क सुरक्षा के लिए नया सुझाव: ट्रक डिज़ाइन में बदलाव आवश्यक है
---
1. भूमिका
भारत में हर साल हजारों सड़क दुर्घटनाएँ होती हैं, जिनमें भारी वाहन जैसे ट्रक एक मुख्य कारण होते हैं। विशेषकर ट्रकों के पिछले हिस्से में होने वाली दुर्घटनाओं में अधिकतर दोपहिया वाहन चालक या पैदल यात्रियों की मृत्यु हो जाती है।
---
2. वर्तमान डिज़ाइन की समस्या
ट्रकों के पिछले टायरों के बीच खाली स्थान रहता है।
यदि कोई व्यक्ति गलती से उस हिस्से में फँस जाए, तो सीधा टायर के नीचे आकर मृत्यु हो जाती है।
वहीं, बस की डिज़ाइन में ऐसा नहीं होता – पूरी बॉडी नीचे तक होती है जिससे फँसने की संभावना कम होती है।
---
3. प्रस्तावित डिज़ाइन समाधान
मुख्य परिवर्तन:
ट्रक को बस जैसी बॉडी दी जाए।
आगे और पीछे के टायरों के बीच "फोल्डिंग पार्टिशन" लगाया जाए।
इस पार्टिशन की ऊँचाई ज़मीन से 600 मिमी तक हो।
अंडररन प्रोटेक्शन (Underrun Guard) को मजबूत और अनिवार्य बनाया जाए।
फोल्डिंग पार्टिशन के लाभ:
ज़रूरत पड़ने पर पार्टिशन को मोड़ा जा सकता है (जैसे सर्विसिंग के लिए)।
आम स्थिति में यह किसी भी व्यक्ति या वाहन को टायर के बीच में फँसने से रोकेगा।
---
4. तकनीकी विशेषताएँ
सामग्री: हल्की लेकिन मजबूत धातु (जैसे एल्यूमिनियम अलॉय)
डिज़ाइन: मोड्यूलर, ताकि सभी ट्रकों पर आसानी से लगाया जा सके
रख-रखाव: कम लागत, फोल्डिंग सिस्टम से आसान उपयोग
---
5. संभावित लाभ
60-80% तक सड़क पर होने वाली जानलेवा दुर्घटनाओं में कमी
विशेषकर दोपहिया वाहनों, साइकिल चालकों और पैदल यात्रियों को सुरक्षा
भारत की छवि एक सुरक्षा-पहचान राष्ट्र के रूप में बनेगी
---
6. भारत सरकार से निवेदन
इस डिज़ाइन का पायलट प्रोजेक्ट शुरू किया जाए।
सभी नए ट्रकों में यह सुरक्षा फीचर्स अनिवार्य किए जाएँ।
पुराने ट्रकों में भी यह फीचर्स लगाने की योजना बने।
कृपया इस सुझाव को जनहित और जीवन सुरक्षा के रूप में स्वीकार करें।
"मनुष्य की जान अमूल्य है – तकनीक थोड़ी महंगी सही, पर जीवन से ज़्यादा नहीं।"

Distressing Experience with Tata Motors 24×7 Roadside Assistance 
On May 4, 2025, around 10:30 PM, my three-month-old Tata Safari (HR87 T 8310) lost power in a remote village due to a dead battery. Despite numerous calls to Tata’s 24×7 helpline (1800 209 8282), no assistance arrived throughout the night.
Stranded with my family, I arranged a local cab to ensure their safety while I remained in the immobilized car, windows stuck open, enduring rain without food or rest. The next morning, two untrained workers arrived around 9:30 AM but couldn't restart the vehicle and suggested towing. After further delays and unanswered calls, I hired a local mechanic who managed to start the car.
I then drove to Moradabad for battery repairs, only to discover the battery's specific gravity was far below normal for a new car. Similar incidents of brand-new Safaris breaking down and customers relying on social media for help are prevalent online.
Tata Motors, this experience was unacceptable. Loyal customers deserve reliable roadside assistance and trained responders, not a night stranded in the rain. Please investigate and improve your RSA service to prevent such ordeals for others.

Title: Extremely Disappointed with Tata Curvv – Clutch Failed Twice Within a Month!

I bought a Tata Curvv just 6 months ago, expecting quality and reliability from a trusted Indian brand. Unfortunately, my experience has been nothing short of a nightmare. The clutch failed not once, but TWICE within a gap of just 15 days!

When I approached the Tata service center, instead of investigating the issue properly, they conveniently blamed my driving skills — despite the fact that my driving rating is 4.9 and I’ve been driving a Maruti car for the past 13 years without a single clutch issue. Clearly, the problem lies with the quality of parts or manufacturing, not the driver.

To make matters worse, the service center seems more interested in piling up high repair bills rather than solving the root cause. It feels like they’re trying to make money off a defect instead of taking responsibility for a clearly faulty product.

Tata Motors seriously needs to rethink their quality control and customer service. I never expected such poor reliability and after-sales support. If this is the experience within the first 6 months, I fear what’s ahead.

I’ll be sharing this review across Google, Facebook, Twitter, Instagram, and any platform I can, so that others don’t make the same mistake. Think twice before investing in the Tata Curvv.

Extremely disappointed customer.

Dear Sir/Madam,
I am writing this to express my extreme disappointment and frustration regarding the pathetic and highly unprofessional customer service I have received from your authorized service center, Jagdish Tata, concerning my vehicle – Tata Altroz, registration number MP14 CD1891.
The car was given for accidental repair and remained at your service center for an unreasonable duration of 33 days, which is absolutely unacceptable. Despite this prolonged wait, the issues have not been resolved satisfactorily.
1. Engine Noise: Upon delivery, I noticed that the engine is making strange and unusual noises, which were not present before.
2. AC Actuator Unrepaired: I had clearly informed your team 35-40 days in advance about the malfunctioning AC actuator. Shockingly, this issue was completely ignored and remains unresolved even after such a long service duration.
3. Punctured Tyre Not Returned: To add to the list of grievances, your showroom got my car tyre punctured during the service, and despite multiple reminders, the tyre has not been returned to me yet. It is still lying with your service center, and no action has been taken.
4. Unresponsive Staff: Repeated attempts to contact the concerned team have been in vain. No one is answering the calls, showing a complete lack of accountability and concern for customer issues.
This level of negligence and mismanagement is absolutely shameful and tarnishes the reputation of Tata Motors. I strongly urge you to take immediate and strict action against the concerned team at Jagdish Tata and ensure that all the pending issues – including engine noise, AC actuator repair, and return of the tyre – are resolved without any further delay.
If corrective action is not taken immediately, I will be left with no choice but to escalate this matter further through consumer grievance platforms and social media.
I expect a prompt response and resolution at the earliest.
Sincerely,
[Tushar Talreja]

मैं Tata Tiago EV का एक ग्राहक हूँ और आपके ध्यान में यह अत्यंत गंभीर मामला लाना चाहता हूँ कि मेरे वाहन में 8 से 10 महीनों के भीतर ही Main Battery और AUX Battery दोनों खराब हो गईं, जबकि वाहन अब तक अपने पहले वर्ष में ही था। यह न केवल टेक्निकल डिफेक्ट का मामला है, बल्कि कंपनी और सर्विस सेंटर की जानबूझकर लापरवाही एवं धोखाधड़ी को भी दर्शाता है।
 वाहन विवरण

  मॉडल: Tata Tiago EV

  खरीद तिथि: 02 नवंबर 2023

  सर्विस सेंटर: AUTOPLEX AV, जयपुर

समस्याओं की टाइमलाइन (Chronology of Events)
1⃣ प्रथम बैटरी खराबी (6वें महीने में)

  केवल 6 महीने के अंदर ही Main Battery और AUX Battery दोनों फेल हो गईं।

  गाड़ी कई बार बीच रास्ते में बंद हुई, जिससे मुझे On-Road Assistance लेना पड़ा।

  10 से अधिक बार ORA कॉल किए गए, और 10-15 बार मुझे लोकल लोगों की मदद से गाड़ी को धक्का देकर या टोचन करके घर और सर्विस सेंटर तक पहुँचाना पड़ा।

  इससे मुझे शारीरिक व मानसिक पीड़ा, आर्थिक नुकसान, और सामाजिक अपमान झेलना पड़ा।

2⃣ बैटरी रिप्लेसमेंट (Invoice No: EUPLJP2425001055, Date: 08/08/2024)

  लगभग 9वें महीने में नई Battery रिप्लेस की गई।

  परंतु रिप्लेस की गई Aux Battery भी डिफेक्टिव थी — क्योंकि वह भी अगले 6 महीनों में फिर खराब हो गई।

  यानी 1 वर्ष के अंदर ही दो बार बैटरी फेल हुई।

3⃣ सर्विस सेंटर की लगातार लापरवाही
 6th Service (20/03/2025 – KMs: 31,254)

  मैंने AUX बैटरी की दोबारा खराबी की शिकायत की, लेकिन सर्विस सेंटर ने कहा:

 “आपको गाड़ी चलाना नहीं आता, ऐसे ही चलाइए जब तक चल रही है।”

  उस समय बैटरी वारंटी में थी, लेकिन जानबूझकर नहीं बदली गई।

 7th Service (16/07/2025 – KMs: 39,631)

  फिर शिकायत की, जवाब मिला:

 “कभी-कभी ऐसा हो जाता है, आप चलाइए।”

  इस बार भी बैटरी वारंटी में थी, लेकिन रिप्लेसमेंट नहीं किया गया।
 ऐसा स्पष्ट प्रतीत होता है कि सर्विस सेंटर ने वारंटी पीरियड खत्म होने तक शिकायत को टालने की नीति अपनाई।

 8th Service (11 नवंबर 2025)

  इस बार कहा गया कि:

 “AUX Battery पूरी तरह खराब हो गई है, अब वारंटी खत्म हो चुकी है, नई बैटरी के ₹6,000 देने होंगे।”

  यानी डिफेक्टिव पीस जानबूझकर वारंटी समाप्त होने तक बदला नहीं गया,
 और अब ग्राहक को पैसे देकर तीसरी बार बैटरी खरीदने पर मजबूर किया जा रहा है।

मेरे ऊपर पड़े प्रभाव

  Mental Loss: लगातार परेशानी और अपमान से मानसिक तनाव।

  Financial Loss: बार-बार ऑन-रोड असिस्टेंस, समय और पैसे की बर्बादी।

  Reputation Loss: बार-बार गाड़ी खराब होने के कारण समाज में शर्मिंदगी।

  Safety Risk: बीच रास्ते में गाड़ी बंद होने से जान का भी खतरा रहा।

 मेरी मांगें (Reliefs Demanded)

  Free Replacement of AUX Battery under warranty (Defective Part का पुनः रिप्लेसमेंट)।

  सर्विस सेंटर AUTOPLEX AV, जयपुर की जांच और कार्रवाई की जाए।

  मुझे हुए Financial, Mental और Reputation Loss का मुआवज़ा दिया जाए।

  भविष्य में ऐसी गम्भीर शिकायतों पर Tata Motors EV Division द्वारा Direct Supervision सुनिश्चित किया जाए।

  Consumer Court / Consumer Forum

  Ministry of Consumer Affairs (GoI)

  EV Ombudsman & Auto Regulatory Authorities

**Extremely Disappointed with Tata Nexon 2025 DCA Creative – Urgent Attention Required**
I’m writing this to express my deep dissatisfaction with the Tata Nexon 2025 DCA Creative, which I purchased in March 2025. Merely two weeks after the purchase, I began experiencing serious issues while driving. Since then, I’ve had to visit the Wasan Motors service center in Kandivali West **4–5 times**, and my car has now been with them **since Monday**.
Despite the creation of a WhatsApp group for communication, the team has been **completely unresponsive** and **irresponsible** in their updates. In today’s demanding work schedules, repeated service visits are not just inconvenient—they're unacceptable, especially after a supposed PDI (Pre-Delivery Inspection) was completed before handover.
On **May 8**, the **gearbox was replaced**. And yet, the issue persists. Now I’ve been informed by the manager that there's a problem with the **axle rod bearing**. This raises a serious question: **If there are so many faults emerging one after another, why is the vehicle not being replaced altogether?** Why are temporary fixes being done despite the involvement of Tata Motors engineers?
The level of after-sales service has been nothing short of **shocking**. I have even reached out to @tatanexonofficial on Instagram but received **no response**. For a car that cost me **15 lakh rupees**, this experience is extremely disheartening. It's not just a car—it’s my **hard-earned money**, and this is not what I expected from a brand like Tata Motors.
I’ve informed Wasan Motors to **resolve the issue today itself**—no more delays, no more excuses. I urge Tata Motors to take this matter seriously and **act responsibly** before further trust is lost.

Based on my experience, I find that Tata spare parts lack reliability.
When compared to other brands, Tata vehicles tend to have higher maintenance costs, a higher frequency of part replacements, elevated labor charges, and expensive spare parts. Offering minimal discounts does not significantly alleviate these costs for customers.
Furthermore, I have observed that service advisors are often unable to provide clear and satisfactory explanations for issues encountered with the car. In many cases, they attribute problems to manufacturing defects or known issues, which are then expected to be resolved at the customer’s expense.
It appears that the service center does not take accountability for these recurring problems. The responses provided are often ambiguous and seem designed to confuse customers rather than address their concerns transparently.
Additionally, I would like clarification on why each part replacement incurs a separate labor charge, even when a single mechanic can handle multiple repairs and replacements.
I urge Tata Motors to acknowledge and address the reliability issues associated with its spare parts to enhance customer trust and satisfaction.

Glad you had a good trip but this sets a bad precedent for anyone wanting to do a long trip in a car. You are only jeopardising your life and that of your loved ones with this setup. The rear seatbelts, including those in the third row are necessary since it stops inertia to push your body forward in the event of a crash. The airbags will also protect you if your body is constrained in one place. It ensures the impact is as minimal as possible. When you’re not wearing a seatbelt, you are essentially an arrow shooting out of a bow when the vehicle comes to an abrupt stop in the event of a crash. The point is to not discourage you or your family on taking road trips but this setup is just unsafe. This is for everyone else who would be tempted to try this out thinking it’s a good idea. It’s not. They were just lucky, you may not be. Don’t try your luck unnecessarily.

Hi @life2_wander I wanted to share some thoughts on your recent video. While it might look cool and inspire others to try this setup, putting your head out while the car is moving is risky and could lead to serious issues. It’s also worth noting that such actions might create complications if there’s an accident and an insurance claim is involved.

Even though there aren’t explicit traffic laws addressing this, it’s important to remember that our traffic regulations haven’t been updated in a while. Videos like this could push authorities to introduce stricter rules in the future.
